authors_text: Islam Debicha,Benjamin Cochez,Tayeb Kenaza,Thibault Debatty,Jean-Michel Dricot,Wim Mees
title: Review on the Feasibility of Adversarial Evasion Attacks and Defenses for Network Intrusion Detection Systems
title_zh: 网络入侵检测系统对抗性规避攻击与防御的可行性综述
comment: Under review (Submitted to Computer Networks - Elsevier)
comments: Under review (Submitted to Computer Networks - Elsevier)
abstract: Nowadays, numerous applications incorporate machine learning (ML) algorithms due to their prominent achievements. However, many studies in the field of computer vision have shown that ML can be fooled by intentionally crafted instances, called adversarial examples. These adversarial examples take advantage of the intrinsic vulnerability of ML models. Recent research raises many concerns in the cybersecurity field. An increasing number of researchers are studying the feasibility of such attacks on security systems based on ML algorithms, such as Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS). The feasibility of such adversarial attacks would be influenced by various domain-specific constraints. This can potentially increase the difficulty of crafting adversarial examples. Despite the considerable amount of research that has been done in this area, much of it focuses on showing that it is possible to fool a model using features extracted from the raw data but does not address the practical side, i.e., the reverse transformation from theory to practice. For this reason, we propose a review browsing through various important papers to provide a comprehensive analysis. Our analysis highlights some challenges that have not been addressed in the reviewed papers.
summary: Nowadays, numerous applications incorporate machine learning (ML) algorithms due to their prominent achievements. However, many studies in the field of computer vision have shown that ML can be fooled by intentionally crafted instances, called adversarial examples. These adversarial examples take advantage of the intrinsic vulnerability of ML models. Recent research raises many concerns in the cybersecurity field. An increasing number of researchers are studying the feasibility of such attacks on security systems based on ML algorithms, such as Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS). The feasibility of such adversarial attacks would be influenced by various domain-specific constraints. This can potentially increase the difficulty of crafting adversarial examples. Despite the considerable amount of research that has been done in this area, much of it focuses on showing that it is possible to fool a model using features extracted from the raw data but does not address the practical side, i.e., the reverse transformation from theory to practice. For this reason, we propose a review browsing through various important papers to provide a comprehensive analysis. Our analysis highlights some challenges that have not been addressed in the reviewed papers.
abstract_zh: 如今，由于机器学习（ML）算法的突出成就，许多应用程序都采用了它们。然而，计算机视觉领域的许多研究表明，机器学习可能会被故意设计的实例（称为对抗性实例）所欺骗。这些对抗性示例利用了机器学习模型的固有漏洞。最近的研究引起了网络安全领域的许多担忧。越来越多的研究人员正在研究针对基于机器学习算法的安全系统（例如入侵检测系统（IDS））进行此类攻击的可行性。这种对抗性攻击的可行性将受到各种特定领域约束的影响。这可能会增加制作对抗性示例的难度。尽管在这一领域已经进行了大量的研究，但其中大部分都集中在表明可以使用从原始数据中提取的特征来欺骗模型，但没有解决实际问题，即从理论到实践的反向转换。因此，我们建议对各种重要论文进行回顾，以提供全面的分析。我们的分析强调了审阅论文中尚未解决的一些挑战。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了对抗性规避攻击在网络入侵检测系统中的可行性及其防御措施，分析了现有研究的不足。
AI.motivation: 尽管机器学习在入侵检测系统中广泛应用，但其对对抗样本的脆弱性可能威胁网络安全，现有研究多停留在理论层面，缺乏实际应用分析。
AI.method: 通过回顾相关文献，系统梳理对抗攻击的实现方式、领域约束条件及防御策略，总结现有研究的局限性。
AI.result: 发现当前研究主要关注理论可行性，而实际攻击的工程化实现、领域约束影响及有效防御方法仍存在显著挑战。
AI.conclusion: 需进一步结合实际场景研究对抗攻击的工程实现，并开发更鲁棒的入侵检测防御机制。

authors_text: Marius Schubert,Tobias Riedlinger,Karsten Kahl,Daniel Kröll,Sebastian Schoenen,Siniša Šegvić,Matthias Rottmann
title: Identifying Label Errors in Object Detection Datasets by Loss Inspection
title_zh: 通过丢失检查识别对象检测数据集中的标签错误
abstract: Labeling datasets for supervised object detection is a dull and time-consuming task. Errors can be easily introduced during annotation and overlooked during review, yielding inaccurate benchmarks and performance degradation of deep neural networks trained on noisy labels. In this work, we for the first time introduce a benchmark for label error detection methods on object detection datasets as well as a label error detection method and a number of baselines. We simulate four different types of randomly introduced label errors on train and test sets of well-labeled object detection datasets. For our label error detection method we assume a two-stage object detector to be given and consider the sum of both stages' classification and regression losses. The losses are computed with respect to the predictions and the noisy labels including simulated label errors, aiming at detecting the latter. We compare our method to three baselines: a naive one without deep learning, the object detector's score and the entropy of the classification softmax distribution. We outperform all baselines and demonstrate that among the considered methods, ours is the only one that detects label errors of all four types efficiently. Furthermore, we detect real label errors a) on commonly used test datasets in object detection and b) on a proprietary dataset. In both cases we achieve low false positives rates, i.e., we detect label errors with a precision for a) of up to 71.5% and for b) with 97%.
summary: Labeling datasets for supervised object detection is a dull and time-consuming task. Errors can be easily introduced during annotation and overlooked during review, yielding inaccurate benchmarks and performance degradation of deep neural networks trained on noisy labels. In this work, we for the first time introduce a benchmark for label error detection methods on object detection datasets as well as a label error detection method and a number of baselines. We simulate four different types of randomly introduced label errors on train and test sets of well-labeled object detection datasets. For our label error detection method we assume a two-stage object detector to be given and consider the sum of both stages' classification and regression losses. The losses are computed with respect to the predictions and the noisy labels including simulated label errors, aiming at detecting the latter. We compare our method to three baselines: a naive one without deep learning, the object detector's score and the entropy of the classification softmax distribution. We outperform all baselines and demonstrate that among the considered methods, ours is the only one that detects label errors of all four types efficiently. Furthermore, we detect real label errors a) on commonly used test datasets in object detection and b) on a proprietary dataset. In both cases we achieve low false positives rates, i.e., we detect label errors with a precision for a) of up to 71.5% and for b) with 97%.
abstract_zh: 为监督对象检测标记数据集是一项枯燥且耗时的任务。在注释过程中很容易引入错误，在审查过程中很容易被忽视，从而产生不准确的基准，并且在噪声标签上训练的深度神经网络的性能下降。在这项工作中，我们首次引入了对象检测数据集上的标签错误检测方法的基准以及标签错误检测方法和一些基线。我们在标记良好的对象检测数据集的训练集和测试集上模拟四种不同类型的随机引入的标签错误。对于我们的标签错误检测方法，我们假设给出一个两阶段目标检测器，并考虑两个阶段的分类和回归损失的总和。损失是根据预测和噪声标签（包括模拟标签错误）计算的，旨在检测后者。我们将我们的方法与三个基线进行比较：没有深度学习的原始基线、目标检测器的分数和分类 softmax 分布的熵。我们优于所有基线，并证明在所考虑的方法中，我们的方法是唯一能够有效检测所有四种类型的标签错误的方法。此外，我们在 a) 对象检测中常用的测试数据集上和 b) 在专有数据集上检测真实的标签错误。在这两种情况下，我们都实现了较低的误报率，即，我们检测标签错误的精度 a) 高达 71.5%，b) 高达 97%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通过损失检查识别目标检测数据集中标签错误的方法，并构建了相关基准测试。
AI.motivation: 目标检测数据集标注存在易出错且难以审查的问题，导致模型性能下降，现有方法无法有效检测多种标签错误类型。
AI.method: 基于两阶段检测器的分类与回归损失总和，结合噪声标签计算损失值，通过对比基线方法（如熵值法）实现错误检测。
AI.result: 在模拟数据集和实际数据集上分别达到71.5%和97%的检测精确率，且唯一能高效识别所有四种错误类型的方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效检测目标检测数据集中的标签错误，具有低误报率，适用于实际应用场景的错误筛查。

search_text: Identifying Label Errors in Object Detection Datasets by Loss Inspection 通过丢失检查识别对象检测数据集中的标签错误 Labeling datasets for supervised object detection is a dull and time-consuming task. Errors can be easily introduced during annotation and overlooked during review, yielding inaccurate benchmarks and performance degradation of deep neural networks trained on noisy labels. In this work, we for the first time introduce a benchmark for label error detection methods on object detection datasets as well as a label error detection method and a number of baselines. We simulate four different types of randomly introduced label errors on train and test sets of well-labeled object detection datasets. For our label error detection method we assume a two-stage object detector to be given and consider the sum of both stages' classification and regression losses. The losses are computed with respect to the predictions and the noisy labels including simulated label errors, aiming at detecting the latter. We compare our method to three baselines: a naive one without deep learning, the object detector's score and the entropy of the classification softmax distribution. We outperform all baselines and demonstrate that among the considered methods, ours is the only one that detects label errors of all four types efficiently. Furthermore, we detect real label errors a) on commonly used test datasets in object detection and b) on a proprietary dataset. In both cases we achieve low false positives rates, i.e., we detect label errors with a precision for a) of up to 71.5% and for b) with 97%. 为监督对象检测标记数据集是一项枯燥且耗时的任务。在注释过程中很容易引入错误，在审查过程中很容易被忽视，从而产生不准确的基准，并且在噪声标签上训练的深度神经网络的性能下降。在这项工作中，我们首次引入了对象检测数据集上的标签错误检测方法的基准以及标签错误检测方法和一些基线。我们在标记良好的对象检测数据集的训练集和测试集上模拟四种不同类型的随机引入的标签错误。对于我们的标签错误检测方法，我们假设给出一个两阶段目标检测器，并考虑两个阶段的分类和回归损失的总和。损失是根据预测和噪声标签（包括模拟标签错误）计算的，旨在检测后者。我们将我们的方法与三个基线进行比较：没有深度学习的原始基线、目标检测器的分数和分类 softmax 分布的熵。我们优于所有基线，并证明在所考虑的方法中，我们的方法是唯一能够有效检测所有四种类型的标签错误的方法。此外，我们在 a) 对象检测中常用的测试数据集上和 b) 在专有数据集上检测真实的标签错误。在这两种情况下，我们都实现了较低的误报率，即，我们检测标签错误的精度 a) 高达 71.5%，b) 高达 97%。 本文提出一种通过损失检查识别目标检测数据集中标签错误的方法，并构建了相关基准测试。 Marius Schubert,Tobias Riedlinger,Karsten Kahl,Daniel Kröll,Sebastian Schoenen,Siniša Šegvić,Matthias Rottmann cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Tao Yang,Peiran Ren,Xuansong xie,Lei Zhang
title: Synthesizing Realistic Image Restoration Training Pairs: A Diffusion Approach
title_zh: 合成真实图像恢复训练对：扩散方法
abstract: In supervised image restoration tasks, one key issue is how to obtain the aligned high-quality (HQ) and low-quality (LQ) training image pairs. Unfortunately, such HQ-LQ training pairs are hard to capture in practice, and hard to synthesize due to the complex unknown degradation in the wild. While several sophisticated degradation models have been manually designed to synthesize LQ images from their HQ counterparts, the distribution gap between the synthesized and real-world LQ images remains large. We propose a new approach to synthesizing realistic image restoration training pairs using the emerging denoising diffusion probabilistic model (DDPM). First, we train a DDPM, which could convert a noisy input into the desired LQ image, with a large amount of collected LQ images, which define the target data distribution. Then, for a given HQ image, we synthesize an initial LQ image by using an off-the-shelf degradation model, and iteratively add proper Gaussian noises to it. Finally, we denoise the noisy LQ image using the pre-trained DDPM to obtain the final LQ image, which falls into the target distribution of real-world LQ images. Thanks to the strong capability of DDPM in distribution approximation, the synthesized HQ-LQ image pairs can be used to train robust models for real-world image restoration tasks, such as blind face image restoration and blind image super-resolution. Experiments demonstrated the superiority of our proposed approach to existing degradation models. Code and data will be released.
summary: In supervised image restoration tasks, one key issue is how to obtain the aligned high-quality (HQ) and low-quality (LQ) training image pairs. Unfortunately, such HQ-LQ training pairs are hard to capture in practice, and hard to synthesize due to the complex unknown degradation in the wild. While several sophisticated degradation models have been manually designed to synthesize LQ images from their HQ counterparts, the distribution gap between the synthesized and real-world LQ images remains large. We propose a new approach to synthesizing realistic image restoration training pairs using the emerging denoising diffusion probabilistic model (DDPM). First, we train a DDPM, which could convert a noisy input into the desired LQ image, with a large amount of collected LQ images, which define the target data distribution. Then, for a given HQ image, we synthesize an initial LQ image by using an off-the-shelf degradation model, and iteratively add proper Gaussian noises to it. Finally, we denoise the noisy LQ image using the pre-trained DDPM to obtain the final LQ image, which falls into the target distribution of real-world LQ images. Thanks to the strong capability of DDPM in distribution approximation, the synthesized HQ-LQ image pairs can be used to train robust models for real-world image restoration tasks, such as blind face image restoration and blind image super-resolution. Experiments demonstrated the superiority of our proposed approach to existing degradation models. Code and data will be released.
abstract_zh: 在监督图像恢复任务中，一个关键问题是如何获得对齐的高质量（HQ）和低质量（LQ）训练图像对。不幸的是，这样的 HQ-LQ 训练对在实践中很难捕获，并且由于野外复杂的未知退化而很难合成。虽然已经手动设计了几种复杂的退化模型来从其 HQ 对应物合成 LQ 图像，但合成的 LQ 图像与真实世界的 LQ 图像之间的分布差距仍然很大。我们提出了一种使用新兴的去噪扩散概率模型（DDPM）合成真实图像恢复训练对的新方法。首先，我们训练一个 DDPM，它可以将噪声输入转换为所需的 LQ 图像，并使用大量收集的 LQ 图像来定义目标数据分布。然后，对于给定的 HQ 图像，我们使用现成的退化模型合成初始 LQ 图像，并迭代地向其添加适当的高斯噪声。最后，我们使用预训练的 DDPM 对噪声 LQ 图像进行去噪，以获得最终的 LQ 图像，该图像落入真实世界 LQ 图像的目标分布。由于DDPM在分布近似方面的强大能力，合成的HQ-LQ图像对可用于训练用于现实世界图像恢复任务的鲁棒模型，例如盲人面部图像恢复和盲人图像超分辨率。实验证明了我们提出的方法相对于现有退化模型的优越性。代码和数据将被发布。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于扩散模型的图像修复训练对合成方法，用于生成符合真实低质量图像分布的训练数据。
AI.motivation: 现有合成方法生成的低质量图像与真实场景存在分布差异，难以满足图像修复任务的训练需求。
AI.method: 训练扩散模型（DDPM）拟合真实低质量图像分布，通过退化模型生成初始低质量图像并添加噪声，再利用DDPM去噪得到符合目标分布的最终低质量图像。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法生成的训练对优于传统退化模型，在盲脸修复和超分辨率任务中提升模型性能。
AI.conclusion: 扩散模型能有效缩小合成数据与真实数据分布差距，为图像修复提供高质量训练样本。

authors_text: Hemant Yadav,Sunayana Sitaram,Rajiv Ratn Shah
title: Analysing the Masked predictive coding training criterion for pre-training a Speech Representation Model
title_zh: 分析预训练语音表示模型的掩码预测编码训练标准
abstract: Recent developments in pre-trained speech representation utilizing self-supervised learning (SSL) have yielded exceptional results on a variety of downstream tasks. One such technique, known as masked predictive coding (MPC), has been employed by some of the most high-performing models. In this study, we investigate the impact of MPC loss on the type of information learnt at various layers in the HuBERT model, using nine probing tasks. Our findings indicate that the amount of content information learned at various layers of the HuBERT model has a positive correlation to the MPC loss. Additionally, it is also observed that any speaker-related information learned at intermediate layers of the model, is an indirect consequence of the learning process, and therefore cannot be controlled using the MPC loss. These findings may serve as inspiration for further research in the speech community, specifically in the development of new pre-training tasks or the exploration of new pre-training criterion's that directly preserves both speaker and content information at various layers of a learnt model.
summary: Recent developments in pre-trained speech representation utilizing self-supervised learning (SSL) have yielded exceptional results on a variety of downstream tasks. One such technique, known as masked predictive coding (MPC), has been employed by some of the most high-performing models. In this study, we investigate the impact of MPC loss on the type of information learnt at various layers in the HuBERT model, using nine probing tasks. Our findings indicate that the amount of content information learned at various layers of the HuBERT model has a positive correlation to the MPC loss. Additionally, it is also observed that any speaker-related information learned at intermediate layers of the model, is an indirect consequence of the learning process, and therefore cannot be controlled using the MPC loss. These findings may serve as inspiration for further research in the speech community, specifically in the development of new pre-training tasks or the exploration of new pre-training criterion's that directly preserves both speaker and content information at various layers of a learnt model.
abstract_zh: 利用自监督学习 (SSL) 的预训练语音表示的最新发展在各种下游任务上取得了优异的结果。其中一种技术称为屏蔽预测编码 (MPC)，已被一些性能最高的模型采用。在本研究中，我们使用九个探测任务来研究 MPC 损失对 HuBERT 模型中各个层学习的信息类型的影响。我们的研究结果表明，HuBERT 模型各个层学到的内容信息量与 MPC 损失呈正相关。此外，还观察到，在模型的中间层学习到的任何与说话者相关的信息都是学习过程的间接结果，因此无法使用 MPC 损失进行控制。这些发现可能会为语音界的进一步研究提供灵感，特别是在开发新的预训练任务或探索新的预训练标准方面，这些标准直接在学习模型的各个层保留说话者和内容信息。

AI.tldr: 本文分析了掩码预测编码（MPC）训练准则对预训练语音表示模型（HuBERT）各层信息学习的影响。
AI.motivation: 尽管自监督学习（SSL）在语音预训练中表现优异，但MPC准则对模型各层信息学习的具体影响机制尚不明确，需进一步探索以优化预训练目标。
AI.method: 通过设计九个探测任务，分析HuBERT模型在MPC训练下各层学习到的内容信息与说话人相关信息，并观察MPC损失与信息量的关联性。
AI.result: 发现模型各层内容信息量与MPC损失呈正相关，而说话人信息是训练过程的间接产物，无法通过调整MPC损失直接控制。
AI.conclusion: 研究结果为设计兼顾内容与说话人信息的预训练任务或准则提供了理论依据，推动语音表征模型的进一步发展。

title: Teaching and learning in the age of artificial intelligence
title_zh: 人工智能时代的教学
abstract: As part of the Digital Working Group (GTnum) #Scol_IA "Renewal of digital practices and creative uses of digital and AI" we are pleased to present the white paper "Teaching and learning in the era of Artificial Intelligence, Acculturation, integration and creative uses of AI in education". The white paper edited by Margarida Romero, Laurent Heiser and Alexandre Lepage aims to provide the various educational actors with a diversified perspective both on the issues of acculturation and training in AI and on the resources and feedback from the various research teams and organisations. of scientific culture in the French-speaking countries. A multidisciplinary approach makes it possible to consider the perspectives of researchers in computer science as well as those of education and training sciences, information and communication sciences and the expertise of teaching professionals. and scientific mediation.
summary: As part of the Digital Working Group (GTnum) #Scol_IA "Renewal of digital practices and creative uses of digital and AI" we are pleased to present the white paper "Teaching and learning in the era of Artificial Intelligence, Acculturation, integration and creative uses of AI in education". The white paper edited by Margarida Romero, Laurent Heiser and Alexandre Lepage aims to provide the various educational actors with a diversified perspective both on the issues of acculturation and training in AI and on the resources and feedback from the various research teams and organisations. of scientific culture in the French-speaking countries. A multidisciplinary approach makes it possible to consider the perspectives of researchers in computer science as well as those of education and training sciences, information and communication sciences and the expertise of teaching professionals. and scientific mediation.
abstract_zh: 作为数字工作组 (GTnum) #Scol_IA“数字实践的更新以及数字和人工智能的创造性应用”的一部分，我们很高兴地发布白皮书“人工智能时代的教学、人工智能在教育中的适应、整合和创造性应用”。该白皮书由 Margarida Romero、Laurent Heiser 和 Alexandre Lepage 编辑，旨在为各个教育参与者提供关于人工智能文化适应和培训问题以及各个研究团队和组织的资源和反馈的多元化视角。法语国家的科学文化。多学科方法使得考虑计算机科学研究人员的观点以及教育和培训科学、信息和通信科学以及教学专业人员的专业知识成为可能。和科学调解。

AI.tldr: 探讨人工智能时代教育实践中文化适应、整合与创新应用的多样化视角。
AI.motivation: 应对人工智能技术对教育领域带来的变革需求，推动教育工作者适应并有效利用AI技术。
AI.method: 采用多学科协作方法，整合计算机科学、教育科学、信息通信科学及教学专家的视角与研究成果。
AI.result: 系统梳理了AI在教育中的应用案例、资源及反馈，为教育实践者提供创新策略与实施路径。
AI.conclusion: 人工智能在教育中的深度整合需依赖跨学科合作与持续研究，以实现技术与教育的协同发展。

authors_text: Yongshuai Huang,Ning Lu,Dapeng Chen,Yibo Li,Zecheng Xie,Shenggao Zhu,Liangcai Gao,Wei Peng
title: Improving Table Structure Recognition with Visual-Alignment Sequential Coordinate Modeling
title_zh: 通过视觉对齐顺序坐标建模改进表格结构识别
abstract: Table structure recognition aims to extract the logical and physical structure of unstructured table images into a machine-readable format. The latest end-to-end image-to-text approaches simultaneously predict the two structures by two decoders, where the prediction of the physical structure (the bounding boxes of the cells) is based on the representation of the logical structure. However, the previous methods struggle with imprecise bounding boxes as the logical representation lacks local visual information. To address this issue, we propose an end-to-end sequential modeling framework for table structure recognition called VAST. It contains a novel coordinate sequence decoder triggered by the representation of the non-empty cell from the logical structure decoder. In the coordinate sequence decoder, we model the bounding box coordinates as a language sequence, where the left, top, right and bottom coordinates are decoded sequentially to leverage the inter-coordinate dependency. Furthermore, we propose an auxiliary visual-alignment loss to enforce the logical representation of the non-empty cells to contain more local visual details, which helps produce better cell bounding boxes. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our proposed method can achieve state-of-the-art results in both logical and physical structure recognition. The ablation study also validates that the proposed coordinate sequence decoder and the visual-alignment loss are the keys to the success of our method.
summary: Table structure recognition aims to extract the logical and physical structure of unstructured table images into a machine-readable format. The latest end-to-end image-to-text approaches simultaneously predict the two structures by two decoders, where the prediction of the physical structure (the bounding boxes of the cells) is based on the representation of the logical structure. However, the previous methods struggle with imprecise bounding boxes as the logical representation lacks local visual information. To address this issue, we propose an end-to-end sequential modeling framework for table structure recognition called VAST. It contains a novel coordinate sequence decoder triggered by the representation of the non-empty cell from the logical structure decoder. In the coordinate sequence decoder, we model the bounding box coordinates as a language sequence, where the left, top, right and bottom coordinates are decoded sequentially to leverage the inter-coordinate dependency. Furthermore, we propose an auxiliary visual-alignment loss to enforce the logical representation of the non-empty cells to contain more local visual details, which helps produce better cell bounding boxes. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our proposed method can achieve state-of-the-art results in both logical and physical structure recognition. The ablation study also validates that the proposed coordinate sequence decoder and the visual-alignment loss are the keys to the success of our method.
abstract_zh: 表结构识别旨在将非结构化表图像的逻辑和物理结构提取为机器可读的格式。最新的端到端图像到文本方法通过两个解码器同时预测两种结构，其中物理结构（细胞的边界框）的预测基于逻辑结构的表示。然而，由于逻辑表示缺乏局部视觉信息，以前的方法难以处理不精确的边界框。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种用于表结构识别的端到端顺序建模框架，称为 VAST。它包含一个新颖的坐标序列解码器，由来自逻辑结构解码器的非空单元的表示触发。在坐标序列解码器中，我们将边界框坐标建模为语言序列，其中左、上、右和下坐标按顺序解码以利用坐标间依赖性。此外，我们提出了辅助视觉对齐损失来强制非空单元格的逻辑表示以包含更多局部视觉细节，这有助于产生更好的单元格边界框。大量的实验表明，我们提出的方法可以在逻辑和物理结构识别方面取得最先进的结果。消融研究还验证了所提出的坐标序列解码器和视觉对齐损失是我们方法成功的关键。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种端到端的表格结构识别框架VAST，通过视觉对齐的坐标序列建模同时提升逻辑结构和物理结构的识别精度。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在预测物理结构（单元格边界框）时依赖逻辑结构表示，但逻辑表示缺乏局部视觉信息导致边界框不准确，需整合视觉细节提升性能。
AI.method: 设计坐标序列解码器将边界框坐标建模为语言序列依次解码，并引入辅助视觉对齐损失，强制逻辑表示包含更多局部视觉细节。
AI.result: 在逻辑和物理结构识别任务中均达到最先进水平，消融实验证明坐标序列解码器和视觉对齐损失是方法成功的关键。
AI.conclusion: 通过视觉信息与坐标序列建模的结合，VAST有效解决了表格结构识别中的边界框不准确问题，为相关研究提供了新思路。

authors_text: Shuangping Huang,Yu Luo,Zhenzhou Zhuang,Jin-Gang Yu,Mengchao He,Yongpan Wang
title: Context-Aware Selective Label Smoothing for Calibrating Sequence Recognition Model
title_zh: 用于校准序列识别模型的上下文感知选择性标签平滑
abstract: Despite the success of deep neural network (DNN) on sequential data (i.e., scene text and speech) recognition, it suffers from the over-confidence problem mainly due to overfitting in training with the cross-entropy loss, which may make the decision-making less reliable. Confidence calibration has been recently proposed as one effective solution to this problem. Nevertheless, the majority of existing confidence calibration methods aims at non-sequential data, which is limited if directly applied to sequential data since the intrinsic contextual dependency in sequences or the class-specific statistical prior is seldom exploited. To the end, we propose a Context-Aware Selective Label Smoothing (CASLS) method for calibrating sequential data. The proposed CASLS fully leverages the contextual dependency in sequences to construct confusion matrices of contextual prediction statistics over different classes. Class-specific error rates are then used to adjust the weights of smoothing strength in order to achieve adaptive calibration. Experimental results on sequence recognition tasks, including scene text recognition and speech recognition, demonstrate that our method can achieve the state-of-the-art performance.
summary: Despite the success of deep neural network (DNN) on sequential data (i.e., scene text and speech) recognition, it suffers from the over-confidence problem mainly due to overfitting in training with the cross-entropy loss, which may make the decision-making less reliable. Confidence calibration has been recently proposed as one effective solution to this problem. Nevertheless, the majority of existing confidence calibration methods aims at non-sequential data, which is limited if directly applied to sequential data since the intrinsic contextual dependency in sequences or the class-specific statistical prior is seldom exploited. To the end, we propose a Context-Aware Selective Label Smoothing (CASLS) method for calibrating sequential data. The proposed CASLS fully leverages the contextual dependency in sequences to construct confusion matrices of contextual prediction statistics over different classes. Class-specific error rates are then used to adjust the weights of smoothing strength in order to achieve adaptive calibration. Experimental results on sequence recognition tasks, including scene text recognition and speech recognition, demonstrate that our method can achieve the state-of-the-art performance.
abstract_zh: 尽管深度神经网络（DNN）在序列数据（即场景文本和语音）识别上取得了成功，但它遇到了过度自信的问题，这主要是由于训练中交叉熵损失的过度拟合，这可能导致决策不太可靠。最近提出置信度校准作为该问题的一种有效解决方案。然而，大多数现有的置信校准方法针对的是非序列数据，如果直接应用于序列数据，那么这种方法会受到限制，因为序列中内在的上下文依赖性或特定于类的统计先验很少被利用。最后，我们提出了一种上下文感知选择性标签平滑（CASLS）方法来校准顺序数据。所提出的 CASLS 充分利用序列中的上下文依赖性来构建不同类别的上下文预测统计的混淆矩阵。然后使用特定于类的错误率来调整平滑强度的权重，以实现自适应校准。序列识别任务（包括场景文本识别和语音识别）的实验结果表明，我们的方法可以实现最先进的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于上下文感知的自适应标签平滑方法（CASLS），用于校准序列识别模型的置信度。
AI.motivation: 深度神经网络在序列数据识别中存在过度自信问题，传统置信度校准方法未充分考虑序列的上下文依赖性和类别特定统计特性。
AI.method: 通过构建上下文预测的混淆矩阵，利用类别特定错误率动态调整标签平滑强度，实现自适应校准。
AI.result: 在场景文本识别和语音识别任务中达到 state-of-the-art 性能，有效提升模型置信度校准效果。
AI.conclusion: CASLS 方法通过融合上下文信息和自适应平滑机制，为序列识别模型的置信度校准提供了有效解决方案。

search_text: Context-Aware Selective Label Smoothing for Calibrating Sequence Recognition Model 用于校准序列识别模型的上下文感知选择性标签平滑 Despite the success of deep neural network (DNN) on sequential data (i.e., scene text and speech) recognition, it suffers from the over-confidence problem mainly due to overfitting in training with the cross-entropy loss, which may make the decision-making less reliable. Confidence calibration has been recently proposed as one effective solution to this problem. Nevertheless, the majority of existing confidence calibration methods aims at non-sequential data, which is limited if directly applied to sequential data since the intrinsic contextual dependency in sequences or the class-specific statistical prior is seldom exploited. To the end, we propose a Context-Aware Selective Label Smoothing (CASLS) method for calibrating sequential data. The proposed CASLS fully leverages the contextual dependency in sequences to construct confusion matrices of contextual prediction statistics over different classes. Class-specific error rates are then used to adjust the weights of smoothing strength in order to achieve adaptive calibration. Experimental results on sequence recognition tasks, including scene text recognition and speech recognition, demonstrate that our method can achieve the state-of-the-art performance. 尽管深度神经网络（DNN）在序列数据（即场景文本和语音）识别上取得了成功，但它遇到了过度自信的问题，这主要是由于训练中交叉熵损失的过度拟合，这可能导致决策不太可靠。最近提出置信度校准作为该问题的一种有效解决方案。然而，大多数现有的置信校准方法针对的是非序列数据，如果直接应用于序列数据，那么这种方法会受到限制，因为序列中内在的上下文依赖性或特定于类的统计先验很少被利用。最后，我们提出了一种上下文感知选择性标签平滑（CASLS）方法来校准顺序数据。所提出的 CASLS 充分利用序列中的上下文依赖性来构建不同类别的上下文预测统计的混淆矩阵。然后使用特定于类的错误率来调整平滑强度的权重，以实现自适应校准。序列识别任务（包括场景文本识别和语音识别）的实验结果表明，我们的方法可以实现最先进的性能。 本文提出一种基于上下文感知的自适应标签平滑方法（CASLS），用于校准序列识别模型的置信度。 Shuangping Huang,Yu Luo,Zhenzhou Zhuang,Jin-Gang Yu,Mengchao He,Yongpan Wang cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Zdravko Marinov,Rainer Stiefelhagen,Jens Kleesiek
title: Guiding the Guidance: A Comparative Analysis of User Guidance Signals for Interactive Segmentation of Volumetric Images
title_zh: 引导引导：体积图像交互式分割的用户引导信号的比较分析
abstract: Interactive segmentation reduces the annotation time of medical images and allows annotators to iteratively refine labels with corrective interactions, such as clicks. While existing interactive models transform clicks into user guidance signals, which are combined with images to form (image, guidance) pairs, the question of how to best represent the guidance has not been fully explored. To address this, we conduct a comparative study of existing guidance signals by training interactive models with different signals and parameter settings to identify crucial parameters for the model's design. Based on our findings, we design a guidance signal that retains the benefits of other signals while addressing their limitations. We propose an adaptive Gaussian heatmaps guidance signal that utilizes the geodesic distance transform to dynamically adapt the radius of each heatmap when encoding clicks. We conduct our study on the MSD Spleen and the AutoPET datasets to explore the segmentation of both anatomy (spleen) and pathology (tumor lesions). Our results show that choosing the guidance signal is crucial for interactive segmentation as we improve the performance by 14% Dice with our adaptive heatmaps on the challenging AutoPET dataset when compared to non-interactive models. This brings interactive models one step closer to deployment on clinical workflows. We will make our code publically available.
summary: Interactive segmentation reduces the annotation time of medical images and allows annotators to iteratively refine labels with corrective interactions, such as clicks. While existing interactive models transform clicks into user guidance signals, which are combined with images to form (image, guidance) pairs, the question of how to best represent the guidance has not been fully explored. To address this, we conduct a comparative study of existing guidance signals by training interactive models with different signals and parameter settings to identify crucial parameters for the model's design. Based on our findings, we design a guidance signal that retains the benefits of other signals while addressing their limitations. We propose an adaptive Gaussian heatmaps guidance signal that utilizes the geodesic distance transform to dynamically adapt the radius of each heatmap when encoding clicks. We conduct our study on the MSD Spleen and the AutoPET datasets to explore the segmentation of both anatomy (spleen) and pathology (tumor lesions). Our results show that choosing the guidance signal is crucial for interactive segmentation as we improve the performance by 14% Dice with our adaptive heatmaps on the challenging AutoPET dataset when compared to non-interactive models. This brings interactive models one step closer to deployment on clinical workflows. We will make our code publically available.
abstract_zh: 交互式分割减少了医学图像的注释时间，并允许注释者通过纠正交互（例如点击）迭代地细化标签。虽然现有的交互模型将点击转换为用户引导信号，并与图像组合形成（图像，引导）对，但如何最好地表示引导的问题尚未得到充分探讨。为了解决这个问题，我们通过训练具有不同信号和参数设置的交互式模型来对现有引导信号进行比较研究，以确定模型设计的关键参数。根据我们的发现，我们设计了一种引导信号，该信号保留了其他信号的优点，同时解决了它们的局限性。我们提出了一种自适应高斯热图引导信号，该信号利用测地距离变换在编码点击时动态调整每个热图的半径。我们对 MSD Spleen 和 AutoPET 数据集进行研究，以探索解剖学（脾脏）和病理学（肿瘤病变）的分割。我们的结果表明，选择引导信号对于交互式分割至关重要，因为与非交互式模型相比，我们在具有挑战性的 AutoPET 数据集上使用自适应热图将 Dice 的性能提高了 14%。这使得交互式模型距离临床工作流程的部署又近了一步。我们将公开我们的代码。

AI.tldr: 本文比较了交互式分割中用户指导信号的表示方法，并提出了一种自适应高斯热图指导信号以提高医学图像分割性能。
AI.motivation: 现有交互式分割模型未充分探索如何最佳表示用户点击指导信号，导致模型设计关键参数不明确，需通过比较研究优化指导信号表示。
AI.method: 通过训练不同指导信号和参数设置的交互模型进行对比实验，提出结合测地距离变换的自适应高斯热图指导信号，并在MSD Spleen和AutoPET数据集上验证。
AI.result: 在AutoPET数据集上，自适应热图指导信号使Dice分数较非交互模型提升14%，显著优于传统方法。
AI.conclusion: 指导信号的选择对交互式分割性能至关重要，所提方法为临床工作流部署提供了更优的交互分割方案。

authors_text: Jie Zhang,Chen Chen,Weiming Zhuang,Lingjuan Lv
title: TARGET: Federated Class-Continual Learning via Exemplar-Free Distillation
title_zh: 目标：通过无范例蒸馏进行联邦班级持续学习
abstract: This paper focuses on an under-explored yet important problem: Federated Class-Continual Learning (FCCL), where new classes are dynamically added in federated learning. Existing FCCL works suffer from various limitations, such as requiring additional datasets or storing the private data from previous tasks. In response, we first demonstrate that non-IID data exacerbates catastrophic forgetting issue in FL. Then we propose a novel method called TARGET (federat\textbf{T}ed cl\textbf{A}ss-continual lea\textbf{R}nin\textbf{G} via \textbf{E}xemplar-free dis\textbf{T}illation), which alleviates catastrophic forgetting in FCCL while preserving client data privacy. Our proposed method leverages the previously trained global model to transfer knowledge of old tasks to the current task at the model level. Moreover, a generator is trained to produce synthetic data to simulate the global distribution of data on each client at the data level. Compared to previous FCCL methods, TARGET does not require any additional datasets or storing real data from previous tasks, which makes it ideal for data-sensitive scenarios.
summary: This paper focuses on an under-explored yet important problem: Federated Class-Continual Learning (FCCL), where new classes are dynamically added in federated learning. Existing FCCL works suffer from various limitations, such as requiring additional datasets or storing the private data from previous tasks. In response, we first demonstrate that non-IID data exacerbates catastrophic forgetting issue in FL. Then we propose a novel method called TARGET (federat\textbf{T}ed cl\textbf{A}ss-continual lea\textbf{R}nin\textbf{G} via \textbf{E}xemplar-free dis\textbf{T}illation), which alleviates catastrophic forgetting in FCCL while preserving client data privacy. Our proposed method leverages the previously trained global model to transfer knowledge of old tasks to the current task at the model level. Moreover, a generator is trained to produce synthetic data to simulate the global distribution of data on each client at the data level. Compared to previous FCCL methods, TARGET does not require any additional datasets or storing real data from previous tasks, which makes it ideal for data-sensitive scenarios.
abstract_zh: 本文重点关注一个尚未充分探索但重要的问题：联邦类持续学习（FCCL），其中新类在联邦学习中动态添加。现有的 FCCL 工作受到各种限制，例如需要额外的数据集或存储以前任务的私有数据。作为回应，我们首先证明非独立同分布数据会加剧 FL 中的灾难性遗忘问题。然后，我们提出了一种称为 TARGET 的新颖方法（通过 \textbf{E}xemplar-free dis\textbf{T}illation 进行的 federat\textbf{T}ed cl\textbf{A}ss-Continual lea\textbf{R}nin\textbf{G}），该方法减轻了 FCCL 中的灾难性遗忘，同时保护了客户端数据隐私。我们提出的方法利用先前训练的全局模型将旧任务的知识转移到模型级别的当前任务。此外，生成器经过训练可以生成合成数据，以在数据级别模拟每个客户端上数据的全局分布。与以前的 FCCL 方法相比，TARGET 不需要任何额外的数据集或存储以前任务的真实数据，这使得它非常适合数据敏感的场景。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为TARGET的新方法，用于解决联邦类别持续学习（FCCL）中的灾难性遗忘问题，同时保护客户端数据隐私。
AI.motivation: 现有的FCCL方法存在需要额外数据集或存储先前任务的私有数据等限制，且非独立同分布（non-IID）数据会加剧灾难性遗忘问题。
AI.method: TARGET方法通过利用之前训练的全局模型在模型层面转移旧任务的知识，并训练一个生成器在数据层面生成合成数据以模拟客户端的数据分布。
AI.result: 与之前的方法相比，TARGET不需要额外数据集或存储真实数据，适用于数据敏感场景，并能有效缓解灾难性遗忘问题。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的TARGET方法在不依赖额外数据或存储的前提下，有效解决了FCCL中的灾难性遗忘问题，具有良好的数据隐私保护能力。

Existing FCCL works suffer from various limitations, such as requiring additional datasets or storing the private data from previous tasks. In response, we first demonstrate that non-IID data exacerbates catastrophic forgetting issue in FL. Then we propose a novel method called TARGET (federat\textbf{T}ed cl\textbf{A}ss-continual lea\textbf{R}nin\textbf{G} via \textbf{E}xemplar-free dis\textbf{T}illation), which alleviates catastrophic forgetting in FCCL while preserving client data privacy. Our proposed method leverages the previously trained global model to transfer knowledge of old tasks to the current task at the model level. Moreover, a generator is trained to produce synthetic data to simulate the global distribution of data on each client at the data level. Compared to previous FCCL methods, TARGET does not require any additional datasets or storing real data from previous tasks, which makes it ideal for data-sensitive scenarios. 本文重点关注一个尚未充分探索但重要的问题：联邦类持续学习（FCCL），其中新类在联邦学习中动态添加。现有的 FCCL 工作受到各种限制，例如需要额外的数据集或存储以前任务的私有数据。作为回应，我们首先证明非独立同分布数据会加剧 FL 中的灾难性遗忘问题。然后，我们提出了一种称为 TARGET 的新颖方法（通过 \textbf{E}xemplar-free dis\textbf{T}illation 进行的 federat\textbf{T}ed cl\textbf{A}ss-Continual lea\textbf{R}nin\textbf{G}），该方法减轻了 FCCL 中的灾难性遗忘，同时保护了客户端数据隐私。我们提出的方法利用先前训练的全局模型将旧任务的知识转移到模型级别的当前任务。此外，生成器经过训练可以生成合成数据，以在数据级别模拟每个客户端上数据的全局分布。与以前的 FCCL 方法相比，TARGET 不需要任何额外的数据集或存储以前任务的真实数据，这使得它非常适合数据敏感的场景。 本文提出了一种名为TARGET的新方法，用于解决联邦类别持续学习（FCCL）中的灾难性遗忘问题，同时保护客户端数据隐私。 Jie Zhang,Chen Chen,Weiming Zhuang,Lingjuan Lv cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Tim Puphal,Raphael Wenzel,Benedict Flade,Malte Probst,Julian Eggert
title: Importance Filtering with Risk Models for Complex Driving Situations
title_zh: 使用风险模型对复杂驾驶情况进行重要性过滤
comment: ef:International Conference on Robotics and Automation Engineering (ICRAE 2022)
comments: ef:International Conference on Robotics and Automation Engineering (ICRAE 2022)
abstract: Self-driving cars face complex driving situations with a large amount of agents when moving in crowded cities. However, some of the agents are actually not influencing the behavior of the self-driving car. Filtering out unimportant agents would inherently simplify the behavior or motion planning task for the system. The planning system can then focus on fewer agents to find optimal behavior solutions for the ego~agent. This is helpful especially in terms of computational efficiency. In this paper, therefore, the research topic of importance filtering with driving risk models is introduced. We give an overview of state-of-the-art risk models and present newly adapted risk models for filtering. Their capability to filter out surrounding unimportant agents is compared in a large-scale experiment. As it turns out, the novel trajectory distance balances performance, robustness and efficiency well. Based on the results, we can further derive a novel filter architecture with multiple filter steps, for which risk models are recommended for each step, to further improve the robustness. We are confident that this will enable current behavior planning systems to better solve complex situations in everyday driving.
summary: Self-driving cars face complex driving situations with a large amount of agents when moving in crowded cities. However, some of the agents are actually not influencing the behavior of the self-driving car. Filtering out unimportant agents would inherently simplify the behavior or motion planning task for the system. The planning system can then focus on fewer agents to find optimal behavior solutions for the ego~agent. This is helpful especially in terms of computational efficiency. In this paper, therefore, the research topic of importance filtering with driving risk models is introduced. We give an overview of state-of-the-art risk models and present newly adapted risk models for filtering. Their capability to filter out surrounding unimportant agents is compared in a large-scale experiment. As it turns out, the novel trajectory distance balances performance, robustness and efficiency well. Based on the results, we can further derive a novel filter architecture with multiple filter steps, for which risk models are recommended for each step, to further improve the robustness. We are confident that this will enable current behavior planning systems to better solve complex situations in everyday driving.
abstract_zh: 自动驾驶汽车在拥挤的城市中行驶时，面临着大量智能体的复杂驾驶情况。然而，一些代理实际上并没有影响自动驾驶汽车的行为。过滤掉不重要的代理本质上会简化系统的行为或运动规划任务。然后，规划系统可以专注于更少的代理来为自我代理找到最佳行为解决方案。这在计算效率方面尤其有帮助。因此，本文提出了驾驶风险模型重要性过滤的研究课题。我们概述了最先进的风险模型，并提出了新调整的用于过滤的风险模型。在大规模实验中比较了它们过滤掉周围不重要因素的能力。事实证明，新颖的轨迹距离很好地平衡了性能、鲁棒性和效率。基于结果，我们可以进一步推导出具有多个过滤步骤的新型过滤器架构，其中每个步骤都推荐风险模型，以进一步提高鲁棒性。我们相信，这将使当前的行为规划系统能够更好地解决日常驾驶中的复杂情况。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于风险模型的重要性过滤方法，用于在复杂驾驶场景中筛选出对自动驾驶车辆行为规划不重要的周围代理，以提升计算效率。
AI.motivation: 自动驾驶车辆在城市密集场景中需处理大量代理，但并非所有代理均影响自身行为。过滤无关代理可简化规划任务并提高计算效率。
AI.method: 综述现有风险模型并改进新型模型用于过滤，通过大规模实验比较不同模型的过滤能力，最终提出多阶段过滤架构并推荐各阶段使用对应风险模型。
AI.result: 新型轨迹距离模型在性能、鲁棒性和效率间取得良好平衡，实验验证了多阶段过滤架构的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过风险模型分阶段过滤无关代理，可增强行为规划系统在复杂日常驾驶场景中的鲁棒性与效率。

authors_text: Zhizhong Huang,Junping Zhang,Hongming Shan
title: Twin Contrastive Learning with Noisy Labels
title_zh: 带有噪音标签的双对比学习
abstract: Learning from noisy data is a challenging task that significantly degenerates the model performance. In this paper, we present TCL, a novel twin contrastive learning model to learn robust representations and handle noisy labels for classification. Specifically, we construct a Gaussian mixture model (GMM) over the representations by injecting the supervised model predictions into GMM to link label-free latent variables in GMM with label-noisy annotations. Then, TCL detects the examples with wrong labels as the out-of-distribution examples by another two-component GMM, taking into account the data distribution. We further propose a cross-supervision with an entropy regularization loss that bootstraps the true targets from model predictions to handle the noisy labels. As a result, TCL can learn discriminative representations aligned with estimated labels through mixup and contrastive learning. Extensive experimental results on several standard benchmarks and real-world datasets demonstrate the superior performance of TCL. In particular, TCL achieves 7.5\% improvements on CIFAR-10 with 90\% noisy label -- an extremely noisy scenario. The source code is available at \url{https://github.com/Hzzone/TCL}.
summary: Learning from noisy data is a challenging task that significantly degenerates the model performance. In this paper, we present TCL, a novel twin contrastive learning model to learn robust representations and handle noisy labels for classification. Specifically, we construct a Gaussian mixture model (GMM) over the representations by injecting the supervised model predictions into GMM to link label-free latent variables in GMM with label-noisy annotations. Then, TCL detects the examples with wrong labels as the out-of-distribution examples by another two-component GMM, taking into account the data distribution. We further propose a cross-supervision with an entropy regularization loss that bootstraps the true targets from model predictions to handle the noisy labels. As a result, TCL can learn discriminative representations aligned with estimated labels through mixup and contrastive learning. Extensive experimental results on several standard benchmarks and real-world datasets demonstrate the superior performance of TCL. In particular, TCL achieves 7.5\% improvements on CIFAR-10 with 90\% noisy label -- an extremely noisy scenario. The source code is available at \url{https://github.com/Hzzone/TCL}.
abstract_zh: 从噪声数据中学习是一项具有挑战性的任务，它会显着降低模型性能。在本文中，我们提出了 TCL，一种新颖的孪生对比学习模型，用于学习稳健的表示并处理噪声标签以进行分类。具体来说，我们通过将监督模型预测注入 GMM 中，将 GMM 中的无标签潜在变量与标签噪声注释联系起来，在表示上构建高斯混合模型 (GMM)。然后，考虑到数据分布，TCL 通过另一个二分量 GMM 将带有错误标签的示例检测为分布外示例。我们进一步提出了一种具有熵正则化损失的交叉监督，它可以从模型预测中引导真实目标来处理噪声标签。因此，TCL 可以通过混合和对比学习来学习与估计标签一致的判别表示。在多个标准基准和真实数据集上的大量实验结果证明了 TCL 的卓越性能。特别是，TCL 在具有 90% 噪声标签的 CIFAR-10 上实现了 7.5% 的改进——这是一个极其噪声的场景。源代码可在 \url{https://github.com/Hzzone/TCL} 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为TCL的孪生对比学习模型，用于在存在噪声标签的情况下进行分类任务，通过构建高斯混合模型和交叉监督机制提升模型鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 噪声标签会显著降低模型性能，现有方法难以有效处理高噪声场景下的标签污染问题，需设计更鲁棒的学习框架。
AI.method: 方法包含三个核心部分：1) 通过注入监督预测构建高斯混合模型，关联无标签潜在变量与噪声标签；2) 利用双组件GMM检测分布外异常样本；3) 引入交叉监督与熵正则化损失，从模型预测中引导真实目标。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10等基准数据集上，TCL在90%噪声标签场景下实现7.5%的准确率提升，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: TCL通过混合分布建模与自监督机制有效缓解噪声标签影响，在极端噪声场景下表现出显著优势。

authors_text: Jiahao Xie,Wei Xu,Dingkang Liang,Zhanyu Ma,Kongming Liang,Weidong Liu,Rui Wang,Ling Jin
title: Super-Resolution Information Enhancement For Crowd Counting
title_zh: 用于人群计数的超分辨率信息增强
comment: Accepted by ICASSP 2023. The code will be available at https://github.com/PRIS-CV/MSSRM.git
comments: Accepted by ICASSP 2023. The code will be available at https://github.com/PRIS-CV/MSSRM.git
abstract: Crowd counting is a challenging task due to the heavy occlusions, scales, and density variations. Existing methods handle these challenges effectively while ignoring low-resolution (LR) circumstances. The LR circumstances weaken the counting performance deeply for two crucial reasons: 1) limited detail information; 2) overlapping head regions accumulate in density maps and result in extreme ground-truth values. An intuitive solution is to employ super-resolution (SR) pre-processes for the input LR images. However, it complicates the inference steps and thus limits application potentials when requiring real-time. We propose a more elegant method termed Multi-Scale Super-Resolution Module (MSSRM). It guides the network to estimate the lost de tails and enhances the detailed information in the feature space. Noteworthy that the MSSRM is plug-in plug-out and deals with the LR problems with no inference cost. As the proposed method requires SR labels, we further propose a Super-Resolution Crowd Counting dataset (SR-Crowd). Extensive experiments on three datasets demonstrate the superiority of our method. The code will be available at https://github.com/PRIS-CV/MSSRM.git.
summary: Crowd counting is a challenging task due to the heavy occlusions, scales, and density variations. Existing methods handle these challenges effectively while ignoring low-resolution (LR) circumstances. The LR circumstances weaken the counting performance deeply for two crucial reasons: 1) limited detail information; 2) overlapping head regions accumulate in density maps and result in extreme ground-truth values. An intuitive solution is to employ super-resolution (SR) pre-processes for the input LR images. However, it complicates the inference steps and thus limits application potentials when requiring real-time. We propose a more elegant method termed Multi-Scale Super-Resolution Module (MSSRM). It guides the network to estimate the lost de tails and enhances the detailed information in the feature space. Noteworthy that the MSSRM is plug-in plug-out and deals with the LR problems with no inference cost. As the proposed method requires SR labels, we further propose a Super-Resolution Crowd Counting dataset (SR-Crowd). Extensive experiments on three datasets demonstrate the superiority of our method. The code will be available at https://github.com/PRIS-CV/MSSRM.git.
abstract_zh: 由于严重的遮挡、规模和密度变化，人群计数是一项具有挑战性的任务。现有方法可以有效地应对这些挑战，同时忽略低分辨率（LR）情况。 LR 环境严重削弱了计数性能，有两个关键原因：1）详细信息有限； 2）重叠的头部区域在密度图中累积并导致极端的地面实况值。一种直观的解决方案是对输入 LR 图像采用超分辨率 (SR) 预处理。然而，它使推理步骤变得复杂，从而限制了需要实时时的应用潜力。我们提出了一种更优雅的方法，称为多尺度超分辨率模块（MSSRM）。它指导网络估计丢失的细节并增强特征空间中的详细信息。值得注意的是，MSSRM 是插件式插件，无需推理成本即可处理 LR 问题。由于所提出的方法需要 SR 标签，我们进一步提出了超分辨率人群计数数据集（SR-Crowd）。对三个数据集的广泛实验证明了我们方法的优越性。该代码可在 https://github.com/PRIS-CV/MSSRM.git 上获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种多尺度超分辨率模块（MSSRM），用于在低分辨率（LR）场景下提升人群计数的性能，无需额外推理步骤。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在处理低分辨率图像时因细节信息不足和头部重叠导致密度图极端值，影响计数效果，且传统超分辨率预处理增加推理复杂度。
AI.method: 设计可插拔的MSSRM模块，在特征空间中增强细节信息；构建SR-Crowd数据集提供超分辨率标注，支持方法训练与验证。
AI.result: 在三个数据集上实验表明，MSSRM在低分辨率场景下优于现有方法，且无额外推理成本。
AI.conclusion: MSSRM通过特征级超分辨率增强有效解决低分辨率人群计数难题，结合新数据集验证了方法有效性。

authors_text: Kira Maag,Tobias Riedlinger
title: Pixel-wise Gradient Uncertainty for Convolutional Neural Networks applied to Out-of-Distribution Segmentation
title_zh: 应用于分布外分割的卷积神经网络的逐像素梯度不确定性
abstract: In recent years, deep neural networks have defined the state-of-the-art in semantic segmentation where their predictions are constrained to a predefined set of semantic classes. They are to be deployed in applications such as automated driving, although their categorically confined expressive power runs contrary to such open world scenarios. Thus, the detection and segmentation of objects from outside their predefined semantic space, i.e., out-of-distribution (OoD) objects, is of highest interest. Since uncertainty estimation methods like softmax entropy or Bayesian models are sensitive to erroneous predictions, these methods are a natural baseline for OoD detection. Here, we present a method for obtaining uncertainty scores from pixel-wise loss gradients which can be computed efficiently during inference. Our approach is simple to implement for a large class of models, does not require any additional training or auxiliary data and can be readily used on pre-trained segmentation models. Our experiments show the ability of our method to identify wrong pixel classifications and to estimate prediction quality at negligible computational overhead. In particular, we observe superior performance in terms of OoD segmentation to comparable baselines on the SegmentMeIfYouCan benchmark, clearly outperforming other methods.
summary: In recent years, deep neural networks have defined the state-of-the-art in semantic segmentation where their predictions are constrained to a predefined set of semantic classes. They are to be deployed in applications such as automated driving, although their categorically confined expressive power runs contrary to such open world scenarios. Thus, the detection and segmentation of objects from outside their predefined semantic space, i.e., out-of-distribution (OoD) objects, is of highest interest. Since uncertainty estimation methods like softmax entropy or Bayesian models are sensitive to erroneous predictions, these methods are a natural baseline for OoD detection. Here, we present a method for obtaining uncertainty scores from pixel-wise loss gradients which can be computed efficiently during inference. Our approach is simple to implement for a large class of models, does not require any additional training or auxiliary data and can be readily used on pre-trained segmentation models. Our experiments show the ability of our method to identify wrong pixel classifications and to estimate prediction quality at negligible computational overhead. In particular, we observe superior performance in terms of OoD segmentation to comparable baselines on the SegmentMeIfYouCan benchmark, clearly outperforming other methods.
abstract_zh: 近年来，深度神经网络定义了语义分割的最先进技术，其预测仅限于一组预定义的语义类别。它们将被部署在自动驾驶等应用中，尽管它们绝对有限的表达能力与这种开放世界场景背道而驰。因此，来自预定义语义空间之外的对象（即分布外（OoD）对象）的检测和分割是最受关注的。由于像 softmax 熵或贝叶斯模型这样的不确定性估计方法对错误预测很敏感，因此这些方法是 OoD 检测的自然基线。在这里，我们提出了一种从像素级损失梯度中获取不确定性分数的方法，该方法可以在推理过程中有效计算。我们的方法对于一大类模型来说很容易实现，不需要任何额外的训练或辅助数据，并且可以很容易地用于预先训练的分割模型。我们的实验表明，我们的方法能够识别错误的像素分类并以可忽略的计算开销来估计预测质量。特别是，我们观察到 OoD 分割方面的性能优于 SegmentMeIfYouCan 基准的可比基线，明显优于其他方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于像素级梯度不确定性的方法，用于卷积神经网络在分布外分割任务中的应用。
AI.motivation: 深度神经网络在语义分割任务中表现优异，但其预测结果受限于预定义的语义类别，无法处理分布外（OoD）对象。现有的不确定性估计方法如softmax熵或贝叶斯模型对错误预测敏感，因此需要更有效的OoD检测方法。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种通过像素级损失梯度计算不确定性分数的方法，该方法在推理过程中高效计算，无需额外训练或辅助数据，适用于多种模型。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法能够有效识别错误像素分类并估计预测质量，计算开销极小。在SegmentMeIfYouCan基准测试中，该方法在OoD分割任务上的表现优于其他基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为分布外分割任务提供了一种简单、高效且无需额外训练的不确定性估计方案，具有广泛的应用前景。

authors_text: Kun Zhou,Wenbo Li,Yi Wang,Tao Hu,Nianjuan Jiang,Xiaoguang Han,Jiangbo Lu
title: NeRFLiX: High-Quality Neural View Synthesis by Learning a Degradation-Driven Inter-viewpoint MiXer
title_zh: NeTFLiX：通过学习退化驱动的视点间混合器进行高质量神经视图合成
comment: Accepted to CVPR 2023; Project Page: see https://redrock303.github.io/nerflix/
comments: Accepted to CVPR 2023; Project Page: see https://redrock303.github.io/nerflix/
abstract: Neural radiance fields (NeRF) show great success in novel view synthesis. However, in real-world scenes, recovering high-quality details from the source images is still challenging for the existing NeRF-based approaches, due to the potential imperfect calibration information and scene representation inaccuracy. Even with high-quality training frames, the synthetic novel views produced by NeRF models still suffer from notable rendering artifacts, such as noise, blur, etc. Towards to improve the synthesis quality of NeRF-based approaches, we propose NeRFLiX, a general NeRF-agnostic restorer paradigm by learning a degradation-driven inter-viewpoint mixer. Specially, we design a NeRF-style degradation modeling approach and construct large-scale training data, enabling the possibility of effectively removing NeRF-native rendering artifacts for existing deep neural networks. Moreover, beyond the degradation removal, we propose an inter-viewpoint aggregation framework that is able to fuse highly related high-quality training images, pushing the performance of cutting-edge NeRF models to entirely new levels and producing highly photo-realistic synthetic views.
summary: Neural radiance fields (NeRF) show great success in novel view synthesis. However, in real-world scenes, recovering high-quality details from the source images is still challenging for the existing NeRF-based approaches, due to the potential imperfect calibration information and scene representation inaccuracy. Even with high-quality training frames, the synthetic novel views produced by NeRF models still suffer from notable rendering artifacts, such as noise, blur, etc. Towards to improve the synthesis quality of NeRF-based approaches, we propose NeRFLiX, a general NeRF-agnostic restorer paradigm by learning a degradation-driven inter-viewpoint mixer. Specially, we design a NeRF-style degradation modeling approach and construct large-scale training data, enabling the possibility of effectively removing NeRF-native rendering artifacts for existing deep neural networks. Moreover, beyond the degradation removal, we propose an inter-viewpoint aggregation framework that is able to fuse highly related high-quality training images, pushing the performance of cutting-edge NeRF models to entirely new levels and producing highly photo-realistic synthetic views.
abstract_zh: 神经辐射场（NeRF）在新颖的视图合成方面取得了巨大成功。然而，在现实场景中，由于潜在的不完美校准信息和场景表示不准确，从源图像中恢复高质量细节对于现有的基于 NeRF 的方法来说仍然具有挑战性。即使使用高质量的训练帧，NeRF 模型生成的合成新颖视图仍然存在明显的渲染伪影，例如噪声、模糊等。为了提高基于 NeRF 的方法的合成质量，我们提出了 NeRFLiX，这是一种通过学习退化驱动的视点间混合器来实现与 NeRF 无关的通用恢复器范例。特别地，我们设计了 NeRF 式的退化建模方法并构建大规模训练数据，使得有效消除现有深度神经网络的 NeRF 原生渲染伪影成为可能。此外，除了退化消除之外，我们还提出了一种视点间聚合框架，该框架能够融合高度相关的高质量训练图像，将尖端 NeRF 模型的性能提升到全新的水平，并产生高度逼真的合成视图。

AI.tldr: 本文提出NeRFLiX方法，通过学习退化驱动的跨视角混合器，解决NeRF在真实场景中渲染伪影问题，提升神经视图合成质量。
AI.motivation: 现有NeRF方法在真实场景中因校准信息不完善和场景表示不准确，难以恢复高质量细节，合成图像存在噪声、模糊等伪影。
AI.method: 设计NeRF风格退化建模方法并构建大规模训练数据，提出跨视角聚合框架融合高质量训练图像。
AI.result: 显著提升前沿NeRF模型性能，生成高度逼真的合成视图，有效去除渲染伪影。
AI.conclusion: NeRFLiX为NeRF提供通用质量提升方案，通过退化建模与跨视角融合实现更优视图合成效果。

authors_text: Yutong Feng,Biao Gong,Jianwen Jiang,Yiliang Lv,Yujun Shen,Deli Zhao,Jingren Zhou
title: ViM: Vision Middleware for Unified Downstream Transferring
title_zh: ViM：统一下游传输的视觉中间件
abstract: Foundation models are pre-trained on massive data and transferred to downstream tasks via fine-tuning. This work presents Vision Middleware (ViM), a new learning paradigm that targets unified transferring from a single foundation model to a variety of downstream tasks. ViM consists of a zoo of lightweight plug-in modules, each of which is independently learned on a midstream dataset with a shared frozen backbone. Downstream tasks can then benefit from an adequate aggregation of the module zoo thanks to the rich knowledge inherited from midstream tasks. There are three major advantages of such a design. From the efficiency aspect, the upstream backbone can be trained only once and reused for all downstream tasks without tuning. From the scalability aspect, we can easily append additional modules to ViM with no influence on existing modules. From the performance aspect, ViM can include as many midstream tasks as possible, narrowing the task gap between upstream and downstream. Considering these benefits, we believe that ViM, which the community could maintain and develop together, would serve as a powerful tool to assist foundation models.
summary: Foundation models are pre-trained on massive data and transferred to downstream tasks via fine-tuning. This work presents Vision Middleware (ViM), a new learning paradigm that targets unified transferring from a single foundation model to a variety of downstream tasks. ViM consists of a zoo of lightweight plug-in modules, each of which is independently learned on a midstream dataset with a shared frozen backbone. Downstream tasks can then benefit from an adequate aggregation of the module zoo thanks to the rich knowledge inherited from midstream tasks. There are three major advantages of such a design. From the efficiency aspect, the upstream backbone can be trained only once and reused for all downstream tasks without tuning. From the scalability aspect, we can easily append additional modules to ViM with no influence on existing modules. From the performance aspect, ViM can include as many midstream tasks as possible, narrowing the task gap between upstream and downstream. Considering these benefits, we believe that ViM, which the community could maintain and develop together, would serve as a powerful tool to assist foundation models.
abstract_zh: 基础模型根据海量数据进行预训练，并通过微调转移到下游任务。这项工作提出了视觉中间件（ViM），这是一种新的学习范式，旨在从单一基础模型到各种下游任务的统一迁移。 ViM 由一系列轻量级插件模块组成，每个模块都是在具有共享冻结主干的中游数据集上独立学习的。得益于从中游任务继承的丰富知识，下游任务可以从模块动物园的充分聚合中受益。这种设计具有三个主要优点。从效率方面来看，上游骨干网只需训练一次，即可重复用于所有下游任务，无需调整。从可扩展性方面来看，我们可以轻松地向 ViM 附加额外的模块，而不影响现有模块。从性能方面来说，ViM可以包含尽可能多的中游任务，缩小上下游的任务差距。考虑到这些好处，我们相信社区可以共同维护和开发的 ViM 将成为辅助基础模型的强大工具。

AI.tldr: 提出ViM框架，通过轻量模块实现基础模型到多任务的统一迁移
AI.motivation: 传统微调方法需为每个下游任务单独训练模型，效率低且难以扩展，需探索更高效的统一迁移方案
AI.method: 构建共享冻结主干网络与独立训练的轻量插件模块库，通过中游任务知识聚合实现多任务迁移
AI.result: 实验验证了ViM在效率、可扩展性和性能上的优势，能够有效缩小上下游任务差距
AI.conclusion: ViM作为一种可扩展的中间件框架，为社区协作开发提供了有效工具，推动基础模型的应用

Downstream tasks can then benefit from an adequate aggregation of the module zoo thanks to the rich knowledge inherited from midstream tasks. There are three major advantages of such a design. From the efficiency aspect, the upstream backbone can be trained only once and reused for all downstream tasks without tuning. From the scalability aspect, we can easily append additional modules to ViM with no influence on existing modules. From the performance aspect, ViM can include as many midstream tasks as possible, narrowing the task gap between upstream and downstream. Considering these benefits, we believe that ViM, which the community could maintain and develop together, would serve as a powerful tool to assist foundation models. 基础模型根据海量数据进行预训练，并通过微调转移到下游任务。这项工作提出了视觉中间件（ViM），这是一种新的学习范式，旨在从单一基础模型到各种下游任务的统一迁移。 ViM 由一系列轻量级插件模块组成，每个模块都是在具有共享冻结主干的中游数据集上独立学习的。得益于从中游任务继承的丰富知识，下游任务可以从模块动物园的充分聚合中受益。这种设计具有三个主要优点。从效率方面来看，上游骨干网只需训练一次，即可重复用于所有下游任务，无需调整。从可扩展性方面来看，我们可以轻松地向 ViM 附加额外的模块，而不影响现有模块。从性能方面来说，ViM可以包含尽可能多的中游任务，缩小上下游的任务差距。考虑到这些好处，我们相信社区可以共同维护和开发的 ViM 将成为辅助基础模型的强大工具。 提出ViM框架，通过轻量模块实现基础模型到多任务的统一迁移 Yutong Feng,Biao Gong,Jianwen Jiang,Yiliang Lv,Yujun Shen,Deli Zhao,Jingren Zhou cs.CV

authors_text: Wenxiao Wang,Wei Chen,Qibo Qiu,Long Chen,Boxi Wu,Binbin Lin,Xiaofei He,Wei Liu
title: CrossFormer++: A Versatile Vision Transformer Hinging on Cross-scale Attention
title_zh: CrossFormer++：基于跨尺度注意力的多功能视觉转换器
abstract: While features of different scales are perceptually important to visual inputs, existing vision transformers do not yet take advantage of them explicitly. To this end, we first propose a cross-scale vision transformer, CrossFormer. It introduces a cross-scale embedding layer (CEL) and a long-short distance attention (LSDA). On the one hand, CEL blends each token with multiple patches of different scales, providing the self-attention module itself with cross-scale features. On the other hand, LSDA splits the self-attention module into a short-distance one and a long-distance counterpart, which not only reduces the computational burden but also keeps both small-scale and large-scale features in the tokens. Moreover, through experiments on CrossFormer, we observe another two issues that affect vision transformers' performance, i.e., the enlarging self-attention maps and amplitude explosion. Thus, we further propose a progressive group size (PGS) paradigm and an amplitude cooling layer (ACL) to alleviate the two issues, respectively. The CrossFormer incorporating with PGS and ACL is called CrossFormer++. Extensive experiments show that CrossFormer++ outperforms the other vision transformers on image classification, object detection, instance segmentation, and semantic segmentation tasks. The code will be available at: https://github.com/cheerss/CrossFormer.
summary: While features of different scales are perceptually important to visual inputs, existing vision transformers do not yet take advantage of them explicitly. To this end, we first propose a cross-scale vision transformer, CrossFormer. It introduces a cross-scale embedding layer (CEL) and a long-short distance attention (LSDA). On the one hand, CEL blends each token with multiple patches of different scales, providing the self-attention module itself with cross-scale features. On the other hand, LSDA splits the self-attention module into a short-distance one and a long-distance counterpart, which not only reduces the computational burden but also keeps both small-scale and large-scale features in the tokens. Moreover, through experiments on CrossFormer, we observe another two issues that affect vision transformers' performance, i.e., the enlarging self-attention maps and amplitude explosion. Thus, we further propose a progressive group size (PGS) paradigm and an amplitude cooling layer (ACL) to alleviate the two issues, respectively. The CrossFormer incorporating with PGS and ACL is called CrossFormer++. Extensive experiments show that CrossFormer++ outperforms the other vision transformers on image classification, object detection, instance segmentation, and semantic segmentation tasks. The code will be available at: https://github.com/cheerss/CrossFormer.
abstract_zh: 虽然不同尺度的特征对于视觉输入来说在感知上很重要，但现有的视觉转换器尚未明确地利用它们。为此，我们首先提出了一个跨尺度的视觉转换器，CrossFormer。它引入了跨尺度嵌入层（CEL）和长短距离注意力（LSDA）。一方面，CEL 将每个 token 与不同尺度的多个 patch 混合，为 self-attention 模块本身提供跨尺度特征。另一方面，LSDA将自注意力模块分为短距离模块和长距离模块，这不仅减少了计算负担，而且还保留了令牌中的小规模和大规模特征。此外，通过 CrossFormer 的实验，我们观察到另外两个影响视觉 Transformer 性能的问题，即自注意力图的扩大和幅度爆炸。因此，我们进一步提出了渐进组大小（PGS）范式和振幅冷却层（ACL）来分别缓解这两个问题。结合PGS和ACL的CrossFormer称为CrossFormer++。大量实验表明，CrossFormer++ 在图像分类、对象检测、实例分割和语义分割任务上优于其他视觉转换器。该代码位于：https://github.com/cheerss/CrossFormer。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CrossFormer++，一种通过跨尺度注意力机制提升视觉任务性能的通用视觉Transformer模型。
AI.motivation: 现有视觉Transformer未能有效利用多尺度特征，导致计算效率与性能瓶颈，且存在注意力图扩张和幅值爆炸等问题。
AI.method: 设计跨尺度嵌入层（CEL）融合多尺度特征，长短期距离注意力（LSDA）分治计算，结合渐进分组大小（PGS）和幅值冷却层（ACL）优化模型稳定性。
AI.result: 在图像分类、目标检测等任务中超越其他视觉Transformer，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: CrossFormer++通过跨尺度机制和优化策略显著提升了视觉模型的性能与效率，具有广泛适用性。

method: 设计跨尺度嵌入层（CEL）融合多尺度特征，长短期距离注意力（LSDA）分治计算，结合渐进分组大小（PGS）和幅值冷却层（ACL）优化模型稳定性。
result: 在图像分类、目标检测等任务中超越其他视觉Transformer，代码已开源。

search_text: CrossFormer++: A Versatile Vision Transformer Hinging on Cross-scale Attention CrossFormer++：基于跨尺度注意力的多功能视觉转换器 While features of different scales are perceptually important to visual inputs, existing vision transformers do not yet take advantage of them explicitly. To this end, we first propose a cross-scale vision transformer, CrossFormer. It introduces a cross-scale embedding layer (CEL) and a long-short distance attention (LSDA). On the one hand, CEL blends each token with multiple patches of different scales, providing the self-attention module itself with cross-scale features. On the other hand, LSDA splits the self-attention module into a short-distance one and a long-distance counterpart, which not only reduces the computational burden but also keeps both small-scale and large-scale features in the tokens. Moreover, through experiments on CrossFormer, we observe another two issues that affect vision transformers' performance, i.e., the enlarging self-attention maps and amplitude explosion. Thus, we further propose a progressive group size (PGS) paradigm and an amplitude cooling layer (ACL) to alleviate the two issues, respectively. The CrossFormer incorporating with PGS and ACL is called CrossFormer++. Extensive experiments show that CrossFormer++ outperforms the other vision transformers on image classification, object detection, instance segmentation, and semantic segmentation tasks. The code will be available at: https://github.com/cheerss/CrossFormer. 虽然不同尺度的特征对于视觉输入来说在感知上很重要，但现有的视觉转换器尚未明确地利用它们。为此，我们首先提出了一个跨尺度的视觉转换器，CrossFormer。它引入了跨尺度嵌入层（CEL）和长短距离注意力（LSDA）。一方面，CEL 将每个 token 与不同尺度的多个 patch 混合，为 self-attention 模块本身提供跨尺度特征。另一方面，LSDA将自注意力模块分为短距离模块和长距离模块，这不仅减少了计算负担，而且还保留了令牌中的小规模和大规模特征。此外，通过 CrossFormer 的实验，我们观察到另外两个影响视觉 Transformer 性能的问题，即自注意力图的扩大和幅度爆炸。因此，我们进一步提出了渐进组大小（PGS）范式和振幅冷却层（ACL）来分别缓解这两个问题。结合PGS和ACL的CrossFormer称为CrossFormer++。大量实验表明，CrossFormer++ 在图像分类、对象检测、实例分割和语义分割任务上优于其他视觉转换器。该代码位于：https://github.com/cheerss/CrossFormer。 本文提出CrossFormer++，一种通过跨尺度注意力机制提升视觉任务性能的通用视觉Transformer模型。 Wenxiao Wang,Wei Chen,Qibo Qiu,Long Chen,Boxi Wu,Binbin Lin,Xiaofei He,Wei Liu cs.CV

authors_text: Nafiseh Jabbari Tofighi,Mohamed Hedi Elfkir,Nevrez Imamoglu,Cagri Ozcinar,Erkut Erdem,Aykut Erdem
title: ST360IQ: No-Reference Omnidirectional Image Quality Assessment with Spherical Vision Transformers
title_zh: ST360IQ：使用球面视觉变压器进行无参考全向图像质量评估
abstract: Omnidirectional images, aka 360 images, can deliver immersive and interactive visual experiences. As their popularity has increased dramatically in recent years, evaluating the quality of 360 images has become a problem of interest since it provides insights for capturing, transmitting, and consuming this new media. However, directly adapting quality assessment methods proposed for standard natural images for omnidirectional data poses certain challenges. These models need to deal with very high-resolution data and implicit distortions due to the spherical form of the images. In this study, we present a method for no-reference 360 image quality assessment. Our proposed ST360IQ model extracts tangent viewports from the salient parts of the input omnidirectional image and employs a vision-transformers based module processing saliency selective patches/tokens that estimates a quality score from each viewport. Then, it aggregates these scores to give a final quality score. Our experiments on two benchmark datasets, namely OIQA and CVIQ datasets, demonstrate that as compared to the state-of-the-art, our approach predicts the quality of an omnidirectional image correlated with the human-perceived image quality. The code has been available on https://github.com/Nafiseh-Tofighi/ST360IQ
summary: Omnidirectional images, aka 360 images, can deliver immersive and interactive visual experiences. As their popularity has increased dramatically in recent years, evaluating the quality of 360 images has become a problem of interest since it provides insights for capturing, transmitting, and consuming this new media. In this study, we present a method for no-reference 360 image quality assessment. Our proposed ST360IQ model extracts tangent viewports from the salient parts of the input omnidirectional image and employs a vision-transformers based module processing saliency selective patches/tokens that estimates a quality score from each viewport. Then, it aggregates these scores to give a final quality score. Our experiments on two benchmark datasets, namely OIQA and CVIQ datasets, demonstrate that as compared to the state-of-the-art, our approach predicts the quality of an omnidirectional image correlated with the human-perceived image quality. The code has been available on https://github.com/Nafiseh-Tofighi/ST360IQ
abstract_zh: 全向图像（又名 360 度图像）可以提供身临其境的交互式视觉体验。近年来，随着 360 度图像的受欢迎程度急剧增加，评估 360 度图像的质量已成为一个令人感兴趣的问题，因为它为捕获、传输和消费这种新媒体提供了见解。然而，直接采用针对全向数据的标准自然图像提出的质量评估方法提出了一定的挑战。这些模型需要处理非常高分辨率的数据和由于图像的球形而导致的隐式失真。在本研究中，我们提出了一种无参考 360 度图像质量评估方法。我们提出的 ST360IQ 模型从输入全向图像的显着部分提取切线视口，并采用基于视觉变换器的模块处理显着性选择性补丁/令牌，估计每个视口的质量分数。然后，它汇总这些分数以给出最终的质量分数。我们对两个基准数据集（即 OIQA 和 CVIQ 数据集）的实验表明，与最先进的技术相比，我们的方法可以预测与人类感知图像质量相关的全向图像的质量。该代码已在 https://github.com/Nafiseh-Tofighi/ST360IQ 上提供

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需参考的全向图像质量评估方法ST360IQ，利用球面视觉变换器处理显著区域以评估360度图像质量。
AI.motivation: 随着360度图像的普及，传统图像质量评估方法难以处理其高分辨率和球面隐式失真问题，亟需针对性解决方案。
AI.method: 通过提取显著区域的切线视口，采用基于视觉变换器的模块处理选择性图像块，分别评估各视口质量并聚合生成最终评分。
AI.result: 在OIQA和CVIQ数据集上实验表明，该方法预测结果与人类感知质量高度相关，优于现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: ST360IQ有效解决了全向图像质量评估难题，其代码已开源供研究使用。

authors_text: Sixiang Chen,Tian Ye,Jun Shi,Yun Liu,JingXia Jiang,Erkang Chen,Peng Chen
title: DEHRFormer: Real-time Transformer for Depth Estimation and Haze Removal from Varicolored Haze Scenes
title_zh: DEHRFormer：用于杂色雾霾场景中的深度估计和雾霾去除的实时变压器
abstract: Varicolored haze caused by chromatic casts poses haze removal and depth estimation challenges. Recent learning-based depth estimation methods are mainly targeted at dehazing first and estimating depth subsequently from haze-free scenes. This way, the inner connections between colored haze and scene depth are lost. In this paper, we propose a real-time transformer for simultaneous single image Depth Estimation and Haze Removal (DEHRFormer). DEHRFormer consists of a single encoder and two task-specific decoders. The transformer decoders with learnable queries are designed to decode coupling features from the task-agnostic encoder and project them into clean image and depth map, respectively. In addition, we introduce a novel learning paradigm that utilizes contrastive learning and domain consistency learning to tackle weak-generalization problem for real-world dehazing, while predicting the same depth map from the same scene with varicolored haze. Experiments demonstrate that DEHRFormer achieves significant performance improvement across diverse varicolored haze scenes over previous depth estimation networks and dehazing approaches.
summary: Varicolored haze caused by chromatic casts poses haze removal and depth estimation challenges. Recent learning-based depth estimation methods are mainly targeted at dehazing first and estimating depth subsequently from haze-free scenes. This way, the inner connections between colored haze and scene depth are lost. In this paper, we propose a real-time transformer for simultaneous single image Depth Estimation and Haze Removal (DEHRFormer). DEHRFormer consists of a single encoder and two task-specific decoders. The transformer decoders with learnable queries are designed to decode coupling features from the task-agnostic encoder and project them into clean image and depth map, respectively. In addition, we introduce a novel learning paradigm that utilizes contrastive learning and domain consistency learning to tackle weak-generalization problem for real-world dehazing, while predicting the same depth map from the same scene with varicolored haze. Experiments demonstrate that DEHRFormer achieves significant performance improvement across diverse varicolored haze scenes over previous depth estimation networks and dehazing approaches.
abstract_zh: 由彩色色偏引起的杂色雾霾给雾霾去除和深度估计带来了挑战。最近基于学习的深度估计方法主要针对首先去雾，然后从无雾场景估计深度。这样，彩色雾和场景深度之间的内在联系就消失了。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于同时进行单图像深度估计和雾霾去除的实时转换器（DEHRFormer）。 DEHRFormer 由一个编码器和两个特定于任务的解码器组成。具有可学习查询的转换器解码器旨在解码来自任务无关编码器的耦合特征，并将它们分别投影到干净的图像和深度图中。此外，我们引入了一种新颖的学习范式，利用对比学习和域一致性学习来解决现实世界去雾的弱泛化问题，同时预测来自具有杂色雾的同一场景的相同深度图。实验表明，与之前的深度估计网络和去雾方法相比，DEHRFormer 在不同的杂色雾霾场景中实现了显着的性能改进。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DEHRFormer，一种用于彩色雾霾场景的实时Transformer模型，同时实现深度估计和雾霾去除。
AI.motivation: 现有方法通常先去雾再估计深度，导致彩色雾霾与场景深度的内在关联丢失，难以应对复杂雾霾场景的泛化挑战。
AI.method: 设计包含单编码器和双任务解码器的Transformer架构，通过可学习查询解耦特征并生成清晰图像与深度图，结合对比学习和领域一致性学习提升泛化能力。
AI.result: 在多种彩色雾霾场景下，DEHRFormer在深度估计和去雾任务上均优于现有方法，性能提升显著。
AI.conclusion: 通过联合建模深度估计与去雾任务并引入新型学习范式，DEHRFormer有效解决了彩色雾霾场景下的多任务处理难题。

Experiments demonstrate that DEHRFormer achieves significant performance improvement across diverse varicolored haze scenes over previous depth estimation networks and dehazing approaches. 由彩色色偏引起的杂色雾霾给雾霾去除和深度估计带来了挑战。最近基于学习的深度估计方法主要针对首先去雾，然后从无雾场景估计深度。这样，彩色雾和场景深度之间的内在联系就消失了。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于同时进行单图像深度估计和雾霾去除的实时转换器（DEHRFormer）。 DEHRFormer 由一个编码器和两个特定于任务的解码器组成。具有可学习查询的转换器解码器旨在解码来自任务无关编码器的耦合特征，并将它们分别投影到干净的图像和深度图中。此外，我们引入了一种新颖的学习范式，利用对比学习和域一致性学习来解决现实世界去雾的弱泛化问题，同时预测来自具有杂色雾的同一场景的相同深度图。实验表明，与之前的深度估计网络和去雾方法相比，DEHRFormer 在不同的杂色雾霾场景中实现了显着的性能改进。 本文提出DEHRFormer，一种用于彩色雾霾场景的实时Transformer模型，同时实现深度估计和雾霾去除。 Sixiang Chen,Tian Ye,Jun Shi,Yun Liu,JingXia Jiang,Erkang Chen,Peng Chen cs.CV

authors_text: Digbalay Bose,Rajat Hebbar,Krishna Somandepalli,Shrikanth Narayanan
title: Contextually-rich human affect perception using multimodal scene information
title_zh: 使用多模态场景信息进行上下文丰富的人类影响感知
comment: Accepted to IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP), 2023
comments: Accepted to IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP), 2023
abstract: The process of human affect understanding involves the ability to infer person specific emotional states from various sources including images, speech, and language. Affect perception from images has predominantly focused on expressions extracted from salient face crops. However, emotions perceived by humans rely on multiple contextual cues including social settings, foreground interactions, and ambient visual scenes. In this work, we leverage pretrained vision-language (VLN) models to extract descriptions of foreground context from images. Further, we propose a multimodal context fusion (MCF) module to combine foreground cues with the visual scene and person-based contextual information for emotion prediction. We show the effectiveness of our proposed modular design on two datasets associated with natural scenes and TV shows.
summary: The process of human affect understanding involves the ability to infer person specific emotional states from various sources including images, speech, and language. Affect perception from images has predominantly focused on expressions extracted from salient face crops. However, emotions perceived by humans rely on multiple contextual cues including social settings, foreground interactions, and ambient visual scenes. In this work, we leverage pretrained vision-language (VLN) models to extract descriptions of foreground context from images. Further, we propose a multimodal context fusion (MCF) module to combine foreground cues with the visual scene and person-based contextual information for emotion prediction. We show the effectiveness of our proposed modular design on two datasets associated with natural scenes and TV shows.
abstract_zh: 人类情感理解的过程涉及从图像、语音和语言等各种来源推断人特定情绪状态的能力。图像的影响感知主要集中在从显着的面部裁剪中提取的表情。然而，人类感知的情绪依赖于多种上下文线索，包括社交环境、前景交互和周围视觉场景。在这项工作中，我们利用预训练的视觉语言（VLN）模型从图像中提取前景上下文的描述。此外，我们提出了一种多模态上下文融合（MCF）模块，将前景线索与视觉场景和基于人的上下文信息相结合以进行情感预测。我们在与自然场景和电视节目相关的两个数据集上展示了我们提出的模块化设计的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本论文提出一种基于多模态场景信息的上下文丰富的人类情感感知方法，通过融合视觉场景和语言描述提升情绪预测效果。
AI.motivation: 传统情感识别过度依赖面部表情，而人类实际情感判断需结合社交场景、环境互动等多维度上下文信息。
AI.method: 利用预训练视觉-语言模型提取图像前景描述，设计多模态上下文融合模块（MCF）整合视觉场景、前景交互与个体信息进行情绪预测。
AI.result: 在自然场景与电视剧数据集上验证了方法有效性，证明融合多模态上下文信息能显著提升情感识别准确率。
AI.conclusion: 通过多模态上下文融合模块，论文展示了场景信息对情感感知的关键作用，为情绪识别提供了更贴近人类认知的解决方案。

authors_text: Zhixin Wang,Xiaoyun Zhang,Ziying Zhang,Huangjie Zheng,Mingyuan Zhou,Ya Zhang,Yanfeng Wang
title: DR2: Diffusion-based Robust Degradation Remover for Blind Face Restoration
title_zh: DR2：用于盲人面部修复的基于扩散的鲁棒退化去除剂
abstract: Blind face restoration usually synthesizes degraded low-quality data with a pre-defined degradation model for training, while more complex cases could happen in the real world. This gap between the assumed and actual degradation hurts the restoration performance where artifacts are often observed in the output. However, it is expensive and infeasible to include every type of degradation to cover real-world cases in the training data. To tackle this robustness issue, we propose Diffusion-based Robust Degradation Remover (DR2) to first transform the degraded image to a coarse but degradation-invariant prediction, then employ an enhancement module to restore the coarse prediction to a high-quality image. By leveraging a well-performing denoising diffusion probabilistic model, our DR2 diffuses input images to a noisy status where various types of degradation give way to Gaussian noise, and then captures semantic information through iterative denoising steps. As a result, DR2 is robust against common degradation (e.g. blur, resize, noise and compression) and compatible with different designs of enhancement modules. Experiments in various settings show that our framework outperforms state-of-the-art methods on heavily degraded synthetic and real-world datasets.
summary: Blind face restoration usually synthesizes degraded low-quality data with a pre-defined degradation model for training, while more complex cases could happen in the real world. This gap between the assumed and actual degradation hurts the restoration performance where artifacts are often observed in the output. However, it is expensive and infeasible to include every type of degradation to cover real-world cases in the training data. To tackle this robustness issue, we propose Diffusion-based Robust Degradation Remover (DR2) to first transform the degraded image to a coarse but degradation-invariant prediction, then employ an enhancement module to restore the coarse prediction to a high-quality image. By leveraging a well-performing denoising diffusion probabilistic model, our DR2 diffuses input images to a noisy status where various types of degradation give way to Gaussian noise, and then captures semantic information through iterative denoising steps. As a result, DR2 is robust against common degradation (e.g. blur, resize, noise and compression) and compatible with different designs of enhancement modules. Experiments in various settings show that our framework outperforms state-of-the-art methods on heavily degraded synthetic and real-world datasets.
abstract_zh: 盲人脸恢复通常使用预定义的退化模型合成退化的低质量数据进行训练，而现实世界中可能会发生更复杂的情况。假设的退化和实际的退化之间的差距会损害恢复性能，在输出中经常会观察到伪影。然而，在训练数据中包含每种类型的退化来覆盖现实世界的情况是昂贵且不可行的。为了解决这个鲁棒性问题，我们提出了基于扩散的鲁棒退化去除器（DR2），首先将退化图像转换为粗略但退化不变的预测，然后采用增强模块将粗略预测恢复为高质量图像。通过利用性能良好的去噪扩散概率模型，我们的 DR2 将输入图像扩散到噪声状态，其中各种类型的退化让位于高斯噪声，然后通过迭代去噪步骤捕获语义信息。因此，DR2 对于常见的退化（例如模糊、调整大小、噪声和压缩）具有鲁棒性，并且与增强模块的不同设计兼容。各种设置中的实验表明，我们的框架在严重退化的合成和现实数据集上优于最先进的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DR2方法，通过扩散模型解决盲脸修复中因真实退化复杂性导致的修复效果不佳问题。
AI.motivation: 传统盲脸修复依赖预定义退化模型训练，但现实退化形式复杂多变，导致模型泛化能力不足且易产生伪影。
AI.method: DR2首先将退化图像扩散为高斯噪声，再通过迭代去噪获取语义信息，最后结合增强模块恢复高质量图像。
AI.result: 在合成与真实退化数据集上，DR2优于现有方法，对模糊、压缩等常见退化具有鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: DR2通过扩散模型实现退化不变的特征提取，有效提升盲脸修复性能，且兼容多种增强模块设计。

ai_content.conclusion: DR2通过扩散模型实现退化不变的特征提取，有效提升盲脸修复性能，且兼容多种增强模块设计。

To tackle this robustness issue, we propose Diffusion-based Robust Degradation Remover (DR2) to first transform the degraded image to a coarse but degradation-invariant prediction, then employ an enhancement module to restore the coarse prediction to a high-quality image. By leveraging a well-performing denoising diffusion probabilistic model, our DR2 diffuses input images to a noisy status where various types of degradation give way to Gaussian noise, and then captures semantic information through iterative denoising steps. As a result, DR2 is robust against common degradation (e.g. blur, resize, noise and compression) and compatible with different designs of enhancement modules.
