authors_text: Wenhan Yang,Jingdong Gao,Baharan Mirzasoleiman
title: Robust Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training against Data Poisoning and Backdoor Attacks
title_zh: 针对数据中毒和后门攻击的鲁棒对比语言-图像预训练
abstract: Contrastive vision-language representation learning has achieved state-of-the-art performance for zero-shot classification, by learning from millions of image-caption pairs crawled from the internet. However, the massive data that powers large multimodal models such as CLIP, makes them extremely vulnerable to various types of targeted data poisoning and backdoor attacks. Despite this vulnerability, robust contrastive vision-language pre-training against such attacks has remained unaddressed. In this work, we propose ROCLIP, the first effective method for robust pre-training multimodal vision-language models against targeted data poisoning and backdoor attacks. ROCLIP effectively breaks the association between poisoned image-caption pairs by considering a relatively large and varying pool of random captions, and matching every image with the text that is most similar to it in the pool instead of its own caption, every few epochs.It also leverages image and text augmentations to further strengthen the defense and improve the performance of the model. Our extensive experiments show that ROCLIP renders state-of-the-art targeted data poisoning and backdoor attacks ineffective during pre-training CLIP models. In particular, ROCLIP decreases the success rate for targeted data poisoning attacks from 93.75% to 12.5% and that of backdoor attacks down to 0%, while improving the model's linear probe performance by 10% and maintains a similar zero shot performance compared to CLIP. By increasing the frequency of matching, ROCLIP is able to defend strong attacks, which add up to 1% poisoned examples to the data, and successfully maintain a low attack success rate of 12.5%, while trading off the performance on some tasks.
summary: Contrastive vision-language representation learning has achieved state-of-the-art performance for zero-shot classification, by learning from millions of image-caption pairs crawled from the internet. However, the massive data that powers large multimodal models such as CLIP, makes them extremely vulnerable to various types of targeted data poisoning and backdoor attacks. Despite this vulnerability, robust contrastive vision-language pre-training against such attacks has remained unaddressed. In this work, we propose ROCLIP, the first effective method for robust pre-training multimodal vision-language models against targeted data poisoning and backdoor attacks. ROCLIP effectively breaks the association between poisoned image-caption pairs by considering a relatively large and varying pool of random captions, and matching every image with the text that is most similar to it in the pool instead of its own caption, every few epochs.It also leverages image and text augmentations to further strengthen the defense and improve the performance of the model. Our extensive experiments show that ROCLIP renders state-of-the-art targeted data poisoning and backdoor attacks ineffective during pre-training CLIP models. In particular, ROCLIP decreases the success rate for targeted data poisoning attacks from 93.75% to 12.5% and that of backdoor attacks down to 0%, while improving the model's linear probe performance by 10% and maintains a similar zero shot performance compared to CLIP. By increasing the frequency of matching, ROCLIP is able to defend strong attacks, which add up to 1% poisoned examples to the data, and successfully maintain a low attack success rate of 12.5%, while trading off the performance on some tasks.
abstract_zh: 通过从互联网上抓取的数百万个图像标题对进行学习，对比视觉语言表示学习已经实现了零样本分类的最先进性能。然而，为 CLIP 等大型多模式模型提供支持的海量数据使其极易受到各种类型的目标数据中毒和后门攻击。尽管存在此漏洞，针对此类攻击的强大对比视觉语言预训练仍未得到解决。在这项工作中，我们提出了 ROCLIP，这是第一个针对目标数据中毒和后门攻击的鲁棒预训练多模态视觉语言模型的有效方法。 ROCLIP 通过考虑相对较大且变化的随机标题池，并每隔几个时期将每个图像与池中最相似的文本而不是其自己的标题进行匹配，有效地打破了有毒图像-标题对之间的关​​联。它还利用图像和文本增强来进一步加强防御并提高模型的性能。我们的大量实验表明，ROCLIP 在预训练 CLIP 模型期间使最先进的针对性数据中毒和后门攻击无效。特别是，与CLIP相比，ROCLIP将定向数据中毒攻击的成功率从93.75%降低到12.5%，将后门攻击的成功率降低到0%，同时将模型的线性探测性能提高10%，并保持类似的零样本性能。通过增加匹配频率，ROCLIP 能够防御强攻击，这些攻击向数据中添加了高达 1% 的中毒示例，并成功保持了 12.5% 的低攻击成功率，同时牺牲了某些任务的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ROCLIP方法，通过增强对比学习过程来提升多模态模型对数据投毒和后门攻击的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有对比语言-图像预训练模型（如CLIP）因依赖大规模网络数据，易受针对性数据投毒和后门攻击影响，而现有研究尚未有效解决这一安全问题。
AI.method: ROCLIP通过引入随机大容量文本池，定期将图像与池中语义最匹配的文本对齐，而非原始配对文本，并结合图像和文本增强技术，切断恶意样本的关联性。
AI.result: 实验表明，ROCLIP使目标数据投毒攻击成功率从93.75%降至12.5%，后门攻击成功率降至0%，同时保持与CLIP相当的零样本性能并提升线性探针任务表现10%。
AI.conclusion: ROCLIP通过动态文本匹配机制有效防御强攻击，在数据污染比例达1%时仍保持低攻击成功率，为安全预训练提供了新思路。

search_text: Robust Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training against Data Poisoning and Backdoor Attacks 针对数据中毒和后门攻击的鲁棒对比语言-图像预训练 Contrastive vision-language representation learning has achieved state-of-the-art performance for zero-shot classification, by learning from millions of image-caption pairs crawled from the internet. However, the massive data that powers large multimodal models such as CLIP, makes them extremely vulnerable to various types of targeted data poisoning and backdoor attacks. Despite this vulnerability, robust contrastive vision-language pre-training against such attacks has remained unaddressed. In this work, we propose ROCLIP, the first effective method for robust pre-training multimodal vision-language models against targeted data poisoning and backdoor attacks. ROCLIP effectively breaks the association between poisoned image-caption pairs by considering a relatively large and varying pool of random captions, and matching every image with the text that is most similar to it in the pool instead of its own caption, every few epochs.It also leverages image and text augmentations to further strengthen the defense and improve the performance of the model. Our extensive experiments show that ROCLIP renders state-of-the-art targeted data poisoning and backdoor attacks ineffective during pre-training CLIP models. In particular, ROCLIP decreases the success rate for targeted data poisoning attacks from 93.75% to 12.5% and that of backdoor attacks down to 0%, while improving the model's linear probe performance by 10% and maintains a similar zero shot performance compared to CLIP. By increasing the frequency of matching, ROCLIP is able to defend strong attacks, which add up to 1% poisoned examples to the data, and successfully maintain a low attack success rate of 12.5%, while trading off the performance on some tasks. 通过从互联网上抓取的数百万个图像标题对进行学习，对比视觉语言表示学习已经实现了零样本分类的最先进性能。然而，为 CLIP 等大型多模式模型提供支持的海量数据使其极易受到各种类型的目标数据中毒和后门攻击。尽管存在此漏洞，针对此类攻击的强大对比视觉语言预训练仍未得到解决。在这项工作中，我们提出了 ROCLIP，这是第一个针对目标数据中毒和后门攻击的鲁棒预训练多模态视觉语言模型的有效方法。 ROCLIP 通过考虑相对较大且变化的随机标题池，并每隔几个时期将每个图像与池中最相似的文本而不是其自己的标题进行匹配，有效地打破了有毒图像-标题对之间的关​​联。它还利用图像和文本增强来进一步加强防御并提高模型的性能。我们的大量实验表明，ROCLIP 在预训练 CLIP 模型期间使最先进的针对性数据中毒和后门攻击无效。特别是，与CLIP相比，ROCLIP将定向数据中毒攻击的成功率从93.75%降低到12.5%，将后门攻击的成功率降低到0%，同时将模型的线性探测性能提高10%，并保持类似的零样本性能。通过增加匹配频率，ROCLIP 能够防御强攻击，这些攻击向数据中添加了高达 1% 的中毒示例，并成功保持了 12.5% 的低攻击成功率，同时牺牲了某些任务的性能。 本文提出ROCLIP方法，通过增强对比学习过程来提升多模态模型对数据投毒和后门攻击的鲁棒性。 Wenhan Yang,Jingdong Gao,Baharan Mirzasoleiman cs.CV,cs.CL,cs.CR,cs.LG

authors_text: Wan Liu,Qi Lu,ZhiZheng Zhuo,Yaou Liu,Chuyang Ye
title: One-Shot Segmentation of Novel White Matter Tracts via Extensive Data Augmentation
title_zh: 通过广泛的数据增强对新的白质束进行一次性分割
abstract: Deep learning based methods have achieved state-of-the-art performance for automated white matter (WM) tract segmentation. In these methods, the segmentation model needs to be trained with a large number of manually annotated scans, which can be accumulated throughout time. When novel WM tracts, i.e., tracts not included in the existing annotated WM tracts, are to be segmented, additional annotations of these novel WM tracts need to be collected. Since tract annotation is time-consuming and costly, it is desirable to make only a few annotations of novel WM tracts for training the segmentation model, and previous work has addressed this problem by transferring the knowledge learned for segmenting existing WM tracts to the segmentation of novel WM tracts. However, accurate segmentation of novel WM tracts can still be challenging in the one-shot setting, where only one scan is annotated for the novel WM tracts. In this work, we explore the problem of one-shot segmentation of novel WM tracts. Since in the one-shot setting the annotated training data is extremely scarce, based on the existing knowledge transfer framework, we propose to further perform extensive data augmentation for the single annotated scan, where synthetic annotated training data is produced. We have designed several different strategies that mask out regions in the single annotated scan for data augmentation. Our method was evaluated on public and in-house datasets. The experimental results show that our method improves the accuracy of one-shot segmentation of novel WM tracts.
summary: Deep learning based methods have achieved state-of-the-art performance for automated white matter (WM) tract segmentation. In these methods, the segmentation model needs to be trained with a large number of manually annotated scans, which can be accumulated throughout time. When novel WM tracts, i.e., tracts not included in the existing annotated WM tracts, are to be segmented, additional annotations of these novel WM tracts need to be collected. Since tract annotation is time-consuming and costly, it is desirable to make only a few annotations of novel WM tracts for training the segmentation model, and previous work has addressed this problem by transferring the knowledge learned for segmenting existing WM tracts to the segmentation of novel WM tracts. However, accurate segmentation of novel WM tracts can still be challenging in the one-shot setting, where only one scan is annotated for the novel WM tracts. In this work, we explore the problem of one-shot segmentation of novel WM tracts. Since in the one-shot setting the annotated training data is extremely scarce, based on the existing knowledge transfer framework, we propose to further perform extensive data augmentation for the single annotated scan, where synthetic annotated training data is produced. We have designed several different strategies that mask out regions in the single annotated scan for data augmentation. Our method was evaluated on public and in-house datasets. The experimental results show that our method improves the accuracy of one-shot segmentation of novel WM tracts.
abstract_zh: 基于深度学习的方法已经实现了自动白质 (WM) 纤维束分割的最先进性能。在这些方法中，分割模型需要使用大量手动注释的扫描进行训练，这些扫描可以随时间累积。当要对新的 WM 片段（即，未包括在现有带注释的 WM 片段中的片段）进行分割时，需要收集这些新的 WM 片段的附加注释。由于区域标注既耗时又昂贵，因此最好只对新的 WM 区域进行少量标注来训练分割模型，并且之前的工作已经通过将分割现有 WM 区域所学到的知识转移到新的 WM 区域的分割来解决这个问题。然而，在一次性设置中，新的 WM 束的准确分割仍然具有挑战性，其中只有一次扫描对新的 WM 束进行注释。在这项工作中，我们探索了新颖的 WM 片段的一次性分割问题。由于在一次性设置中，带注释的训练数据极其稀缺，因此基于现有的知识转移框架，我们建议进一步对单带注释的扫描进行广泛的数据增强，从而生成合成的带注释的训练数据。我们设计了几种不同的策略来屏蔽单个带注释的扫描中的区域以进​​行数据增强。我们的方法在公共和内部数据集上进行了评估。实验结果表明，我们的方法提高了新型 WM 束的一次性分割的准确性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种通过大量数据增强实现新颖白质束的一次性分割方法。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习方法分割新颖白质束需大量标注数据，而获取标注数据耗时耗力，尤其在仅有一例标注数据的one-shot场景下分割精度不足。
AI.method: 基于知识迁移框架，对单一标注扫描进行多策略区域掩码的数据增强，生成合成训练数据以提升模型性能。
AI.result: 在公开和自建数据集上实验表明，该方法有效提高了新颖白质束的一次性分割准确率。
AI.conclusion: 通过数据增强策略可有效缓解one-shot场景下新颖白质束分割的数据稀缺问题。

authors_text: Yuheng Jia,Jiawei Tang,Jiahao Jiang
title: Label Distribution Learning from Logical Label
title_zh: 从逻辑标签学习标签分布
abstract: Label distribution learning (LDL) is an effective method to predict the label description degree (a.k.a. label distribution) of a sample. However, annotating label distribution (LD) for training samples is extremely costly. So recent studies often first use label enhancement (LE) to generate the estimated label distribution from the logical label and then apply external LDL algorithms on the recovered label distribution to predict the label distribution for unseen samples. But this step-wise manner overlooks the possible connections between LE and LDL. Moreover, the existing LE approaches may assign some description degrees to invalid labels. To solve the above problems, we propose a novel method to learn an LDL model directly from the logical label, which unifies LE and LDL into a joint model, and avoids the drawbacks of the previous LE methods. Extensive experiments on various datasets prove that the proposed approach can construct a reliable LDL model directly from the logical label, and produce more accurate label distribution than the state-of-the-art LE methods.
summary: Label distribution learning (LDL) is an effective method to predict the label description degree (a.k.a. label distribution) of a sample. However, annotating label distribution (LD) for training samples is extremely costly. So recent studies often first use label enhancement (LE) to generate the estimated label distribution from the logical label and then apply external LDL algorithms on the recovered label distribution to predict the label distribution for unseen samples. But this step-wise manner overlooks the possible connections between LE and LDL. Moreover, the existing LE approaches may assign some description degrees to invalid labels. To solve the above problems, we propose a novel method to learn an LDL model directly from the logical label, which unifies LE and LDL into a joint model, and avoids the drawbacks of the previous LE methods. Extensive experiments on various datasets prove that the proposed approach can construct a reliable LDL model directly from the logical label, and produce more accurate label distribution than the state-of-the-art LE methods.
abstract_zh: 标签分布学习（LDL）是预测样本标签描述程度（又称标签分布）的有效方法。然而，注释训练样本的标签分布（LD）成本极高。因此，最近的研究通常首先使用标签增强（LE）从逻辑标签生成估计的标签分布，然后在恢复的标签分布上应用外部 LDL 算法来预测未见过样本的标签分布。但这种逐步的方式忽略了 LE 和 LDL 之间可能存在的联系。此外，现有的LE方法可能会为无效标签分配一些描述程度。为了解决上述问题，我们提出了一种直接从逻辑标签学习 LDL 模型的新方法，它将 LE 和 LDL 统一为联合模型，并避免了以前 LE 方法的缺点。对各种数据集的大量实验证明，所提出的方法可以直接从逻辑标签构建可靠的 LDL 模型，并产生比最先进的 LE 方法更准确的标签分布。

AI.tldr: 提出一种直接从逻辑标签学习标签分布的方法，统一标签增强与标签分布学习过程
AI.motivation: 现有方法分步处理导致连接被忽略，且LE方法可能为无效标签分配描述度，影响准确性
AI.method: 提出联合模型直接从逻辑标签学习LDL，整合标签增强与分布学习，避免无效标签分配
AI.result: 在多个数据集上验证，构建可靠LDL模型，标签分布预测优于现有LE方法

search_text: Label Distribution Learning from Logical Label 从逻辑标签学习标签分布 Label distribution learning (LDL) is an effective method to predict the label description degree (a.k.a. label distribution) of a sample. However, annotating label distribution (LD) for training samples is extremely costly. So recent studies often first use label enhancement (LE) to generate the estimated label distribution from the logical label and then apply external LDL algorithms on the recovered label distribution to predict the label distribution for unseen samples. But this step-wise manner overlooks the possible connections between LE and LDL. Moreover, the existing LE approaches may assign some description degrees to invalid labels. To solve the above problems, we propose a novel method to learn an LDL model directly from the logical label, which unifies LE and LDL into a joint model, and avoids the drawbacks of the previous LE methods. Extensive experiments on various datasets prove that the proposed approach can construct a reliable LDL model directly from the logical label, and produce more accurate label distribution than the state-of-the-art LE methods. 标签分布学习（LDL）是预测样本标签描述程度（又称标签分布）的有效方法。然而，注释训练样本的标签分布（LD）成本极高。因此，最近的研究通常首先使用标签增强（LE）从逻辑标签生成估计的标签分布，然后在恢复的标签分布上应用外部 LDL 算法来预测未见过样本的标签分布。但这种逐步的方式忽略了 LE 和 LDL 之间可能存在的联系。此外，现有的LE方法可能会为无效标签分配一些描述程度。为了解决上述问题，我们提出了一种直接从逻辑标签学习 LDL 模型的新方法，它将 LE 和 LDL 统一为联合模型，并避免了以前 LE 方法的缺点。对各种数据集的大量实验证明，所提出的方法可以直接从逻辑标签构建可靠的 LDL 模型，并产生比最先进的 LE 方法更准确的标签分布。 提出一种直接从逻辑标签学习标签分布的方法，统一标签增强与标签分布学习过程 Yuheng Jia,Jiawei Tang,Jiahao Jiang cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Kijung Lee
title: Why do Tweeters regret sharing? Impacts of Twitter users' perception of sharing risk, perceived problems on Twitter, and the motivation of use on their behavior of regret sharing
title_zh: 为什么推特用户会后悔分享？ Twitter 用户对分享风险的感知、对 Twitter 的感知问题以及使用动机对其后悔分享行为的影响
abstract: This study presents a secondary data analysis of the survey data collected as part of the American Trends Panel series by the Pew Research Center. A logistic regression was performed to ascertain the effects of the perceived risk of sharing, perceived problems on Twitter, and motivation of using Twitter on the likelihood that participants regret sharing on Twitter. The logistic regression model was statistically significant, \c{hi}2(15) = 102.5, p < .001. The model correctly classified 78.5 percent of cases. Whether or not Twitter users regret sharing on Twitter depends on different motivations for using Twitter. We observe that "A way to express my opinion" is statistically significant in the mod-el, indicating that the odds of Twitter users regretting sharing for this motivation is 2.1 times higher than that of entertainment. Perceived risks of potential hostility and visibility were negatively associated with an increased likelihood of regret sharing. In contrast, perceived problems on Twitter concerning misinformation were negatively associated with the likelihood of regret sharing.
summary: This study presents a secondary data analysis of the survey data collected as part of the American Trends Panel series by the Pew Research Center. A logistic regression was performed to ascertain the effects of the perceived risk of sharing, perceived problems on Twitter, and motivation of using Twitter on the likelihood that participants regret sharing on Twitter. The logistic regression model was statistically significant, \c{hi}2(15) = 102.5, p < .001. The model correctly classified 78.5 percent of cases. Whether or not Twitter users regret sharing on Twitter depends on different motivations for using Twitter. We observe that "A way to express my opinion" is statistically significant in the mod-el, indicating that the odds of Twitter users regretting sharing for this motivation is 2.1 times higher than that of entertainment. Perceived risks of potential hostility and visibility were negatively associated with an increased likelihood of regret sharing. In contrast, perceived problems on Twitter concerning misinformation were negatively associated with the likelihood of regret sharing.
abstract_zh: 本研究对皮尤研究中心美国趋势小组系列收集的调查数据进行了二次数据分析。进行逻辑回归以确定感知的分享风险、感知到的 Twitter 上的问题以及使用 Twitter 的动机对参与者后悔在 Twitter 上分享的可能性的影响。逻辑回归模型具有统计显着性，\c{hi}2(15) = 102.5，p < .001。该模型正确分类了 78.5% 的案例。 Twitter 用户是否后悔在 Twitter 上分享内容取决于使用 Twitter 的不同动机。我们观察到“表达我的观点的一种方式”在模型中具有统计显着性，这表明 Twitter 用户因这种动机而后悔分享的几率是娱乐的 2.1 倍。潜在敌意和可见度的感知风险与分享遗憾的可能性增加呈负相关。相比之下，推特上有关错误信息的感知问题与分享遗憾的可能性呈负相关。

AI.tldr: 该论文分析了推特用户分享后产生懊悔行为的影响因素，包括对分享风险的感知、对推特平台问题的认知以及使用动机。
AI.motivation: 研究旨在探讨推特用户因分享内容而产生懊悔行为的心理机制及影响因素，以理解社交媒体使用中的风险认知与行为关联。
AI.method: 采用皮尤研究中心美国趋势面板调查数据，通过逻辑回归分析感知风险、平台问题认知及使用动机对懊悔分享行为的影响。
AI.result: 逻辑回归模型显著（χ²=102.5, p<0.001），正确分类率达78.5%。表达观点的动机比娱乐动机更易引发懊悔（比值比2.1），而对潜在敌意和曝光度的感知风险、虚假信息问题与懊悔概率呈负相关。
AI.conclusion: 推特用户的懊悔分享行为受多重因素影响，其中使用动机（如自我表达）和风险认知（如敌意感知）是关键变量，需结合用户心理与平台特性进行干预设计。

authors_text: Bowen Jiang,Camillo J. Taylor
title: Hierarchical Relationships: A New Perspective to Enhance Scene Graph Generation
title_zh: 层次关系：增强场景图生成的新视角
comment: NeurIPS 2023 New Frontiers in Graph Learning Workshop (NeurIPS GLFrontiers 2023); NeurIPS 2023 Queer in AI Workshop. This paper is a preliminary work of the full paper available at arXiv:2311.12889
comments: NeurIPS 2023 New Frontiers in Graph Learning Workshop (NeurIPS GLFrontiers 2023); NeurIPS 2023 Queer in AI Workshop. This paper is a preliminary work of the full paper available at arXiv:2311.12889
abstract: This paper presents a finding that leveraging the hierarchical structures among labels for relationships and objects can substantially improve the performance of scene graph generation systems. The focus of this work is to create an informative hierarchical structure that can divide object and relationship categories into disjoint super-categories in a systematic way. Specifically, we introduce a Bayesian prediction head to jointly predict the super-category of relationships between a pair of object instances, as well as the detailed relationship within that super-category simultaneously, facilitating more informative predictions. The resulting model exhibits the capability to produce a more extensive set of predicates beyond the dataset annotations, and to tackle the prevalent issue of low annotation quality. While our paper presents preliminary findings, experiments on the Visual Genome dataset show its strong performance, particularly in predicate classifications and zero-shot settings, that demonstrates the promise of our approach.
summary: This paper presents a finding that leveraging the hierarchical structures among labels for relationships and objects can substantially improve the performance of scene graph generation systems. The focus of this work is to create an informative hierarchical structure that can divide object and relationship categories into disjoint super-categories in a systematic way. Specifically, we introduce a Bayesian prediction head to jointly predict the super-category of relationships between a pair of object instances, as well as the detailed relationship within that super-category simultaneously, facilitating more informative predictions. The resulting model exhibits the capability to produce a more extensive set of predicates beyond the dataset annotations, and to tackle the prevalent issue of low annotation quality. While our paper presents preliminary findings, experiments on the Visual Genome dataset show its strong performance, particularly in predicate classifications and zero-shot settings, that demonstrates the promise of our approach.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一个发现，即利用关系和对象标签之间的层次结构可以显着提高场景图生成系统的性能。这项工作的重点是创建一个信息丰富的层次结构，可以系统地将对象和关系类别划分为不相交的超级类别。具体来说，我们引入了贝叶斯预测头来联合预测一对对象实例之间关系的超类别，以及同时预测该超类别内的详细关系，从而促进提供更多信息的预测。由此产生的模型表现出能够生成超出数据集注释的更广泛的谓词集，并解决注释质量低的普遍问题。虽然我们的论文提出了初步发现，但视觉基因组数据集上的实验显示了其强大的性能，特别是在谓词分类和零样本设置方面，这证明了我们方法的前景。

AI.tldr: 本文提出利用对象和关系标签的层次结构来提升场景图生成性能
AI.motivation: 现有场景图生成方法未能有效利用标签间的层次关系，限制了预测能力和泛化性
AI.method: 引入贝叶斯预测头，联合预测关系的超级类别及具体关系，构建系统化的层次结构
AI.result: 在Visual Genome数据集上，模型在谓词分类和零样本设置中表现优异，验证了方法的有效性
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过层次结构提升预测能力，有效应对标注质量低的问题，展现出良好的应用前景

authors_text: Zixiang Zhao,Haowen Bai,Yuanzhi Zhu,Jiangshe Zhang,Shuang Xu,Yulun Zhang,Kai Zhang,Deyu Meng,Radu Timofte,Luc Van Gool
title: DDFM: Denoising Diffusion Model for Multi-Modality Image Fusion
title_zh: DDFM：多模态图像融合的去噪扩散模型
abstract: Multi-modality image fusion aims to combine different modalities to produce fused images that retain the complementary features of each modality, such as functional highlights and texture details. To leverage strong generative priors and address challenges such as unstable training and lack of interpretability for GAN-based generative methods, we propose a novel fusion algorithm based on the denoising diffusion probabilistic model (DDPM). The fusion task is formulated as a conditional generation problem under the DDPM sampling framework, which is further divided into an unconditional generation subproblem and a maximum likelihood subproblem. The latter is modeled in a hierarchical Bayesian manner with latent variables and inferred by the expectation-maximization (EM) algorithm. By integrating the inference solution into the diffusion sampling iteration, our method can generate high-quality fused images with natural image generative priors and cross-modality information from source images. Note that all we required is an unconditional pre-trained generative model, and no fine-tuning is needed. Our extensive experiments indicate that our approach yields promising fusion results in infrared-visible image fusion and medical image fusion. The code is available at \url{https://github.com/Zhaozixiang1228/MMIF-DDFM}.
summary: Multi-modality image fusion aims to combine different modalities to produce fused images that retain the complementary features of each modality, such as functional highlights and texture details. To leverage strong generative priors and address challenges such as unstable training and lack of interpretability for GAN-based generative methods, we propose a novel fusion algorithm based on the denoising diffusion probabilistic model (DDPM). The fusion task is formulated as a conditional generation problem under the DDPM sampling framework, which is further divided into an unconditional generation subproblem and a maximum likelihood subproblem. The latter is modeled in a hierarchical Bayesian manner with latent variables and inferred by the expectation-maximization (EM) algorithm. By integrating the inference solution into the diffusion sampling iteration, our method can generate high-quality fused images with natural image generative priors and cross-modality information from source images. Note that all we required is an unconditional pre-trained generative model, and no fine-tuning is needed. Our extensive experiments indicate that our approach yields promising fusion results in infrared-visible image fusion and medical image fusion. The code is available at \url{https://github.com/Zhaozixiang1228/MMIF-DDFM}.
abstract_zh: 多模态图像融合旨在结合不同模态来生成融合图像，保留每种模态的互补特征，例如功能亮点和纹理细节。为了利用强大的生成先验并解决基于 GAN 的生成方法的训练不稳定和缺乏可解释性等挑战，我们提出了一种基于去噪扩散概率模型（DDPM）的新型融合算法。融合任务被表述为DDPM采样框架下的条件生成问题，进一步分为无条件生成子问题和最大似然子问题。后者以具有潜在变量的分层贝叶斯方式建模，并通过期望最大化（EM）算法进行推断。通过将推理解决方案集成到扩散采样迭代中，我们的方法可以利用自然图像生成先验和源图像的跨模态信息生成高质量的融合图像。请注意，我们所需要的只是一个无条件的预训练生成模型，不需要微调。我们的大量实验表明，我们的方法在红外-可见光图像融合和医学图像融合方面产生了有希望的融合结果。代码可在 \url{https://github.com/Zhaozixiang1228/MMIF-DDFM} 获取。

AI.tldr: 提出基于去噪扩散模型的多模态图像融合算法，生成高质量融合图像。
AI.motivation: 解决基于GAN的方法在训练不稳定性和缺乏可解释性的问题，利用扩散模型的生成先验进行多模态图像融合。
AI.method: 将融合任务作为条件生成问题，分解为无条件生成和最大似然子问题，采用分层贝叶斯建模和EM算法推断，并结合扩散采样过程。
AI.result: 在红外-可见光和医学图像融合任务中取得良好效果，无需微调预训练模型。
AI.conclusion: 提出的方法通过整合推理解到扩散采样中，无需微调即可生成高质量融合图像，验证了其在多模态融合中的有效性。

To leverage strong generative priors and address challenges such as unstable training and lack of interpretability for GAN-based generative methods, we propose a novel fusion algorithm based on the denoising diffusion probabilistic model (DDPM). The fusion task is formulated as a conditional generation problem under the DDPM sampling framework, which is further divided into an unconditional generation subproblem and a maximum likelihood subproblem. The latter is modeled in a hierarchical Bayesian manner with latent variables and inferred by the expectation-maximization (EM) algorithm. By integrating the inference solution into the diffusion sampling iteration, our method can generate high-quality fused images with natural image generative priors and cross-modality information from source images. Note that all we required is an unconditional pre-trained generative model, and no fine-tuning is needed. Our extensive experiments indicate that our approach yields promising fusion results in infrared-visible image fusion and medical image fusion.
